Оно совершает его с {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB|Преимуществом}, если вы или ваши союзники сражаетесь с ним. При провале цель получает состояние {@condition Charmed|XPHB|Очарован} до окончания заклинания или пока вы или ваши союзники не нанесёте ей урон. {@condition Charmed|XPHB|Очарованное} существо {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Дружелюбно} к вам. Когда заклинание заканчивается, цель узнаёт, что была {@condition Charmed|XPHB|Очарована} вами.
Charm Monster.entriesHigherLevel.0.entri: Вы можете выбрать одно дополнительное существо за каждый уровень ячейки заклинания выше 4-го.
Charm Monster.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Charm Monster.name: Очарование чудовища
Charm Person.entries.0: Один Гуманоид, которого вы видите в пределах дистанции, совершает спасбросок Мудрости. Он совершает его с {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB|Преимуществом}, если вы или ваши союзники сражаетесь с ним. При провале цель получает состояние {@condition Charmed|XPHB|Очарован} до окончания заклинания или пока вы или ваши союзники не нанесёте ей урон. {@condition Charmed|XPHB|Очарованное} существо {@variantrule Friendly [Attitude]|XPHB|Дружелюбно} к вам. Когда заклинание заканчивается, цель узнаёт, что была {@condition Charmed|XPHB|Очарована} вами.
Charm Person.entriesHigherLevel.0.entrie: Вы можете выбрать одно дополнительное существо за каждый уровень ячейки заклинания выше 1-го.
Charm Person.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Charm Person.name: Очарование личности
Chill Touch.entries.0: Вы направляете могильный холод на цель в пределах досягаемости; совершите по ней рукопашную атаку заклинанием. При попадании цель получает {@damage 1d10} Некротического урона и до конца вашего следующего хода не может восстанавливать {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Хиты}.
Chill Touch.entriesHigherLevel.0.entries: Урон увеличивается на {@damage 1d10}, когда вы достигаете 5-го ({@damage 2d10}), 11-го ({@damage 3d10}) и 17-го ({@damage 4d10}) уровней.
Chill Touch.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Улучшение заговора
Chill Touch.name: Леденящее прикосновение
Chorus of the Lost.entries.0: Вы призываете жуткую песнь и стенания потерянных душ из точки, которую видите в пределах дистанции. Каждое существо в пределах {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Сферы} с радиусом 15 футов с центром в этой точке должно совершить спасбросок Мудрости. При провале цель получает {@damage 2d6} урона психической энергией и получает состояние {@condition Frightened|XPHB|Испуган} на время действия заклинания. При успехе она получает половину этого урона и состояние {@condition Frightened|XPHB|Испуган} до конца своего следующего хода. {@condition Frightened|XPHB|Испуганная} цель повторяет спасбросок в конце каждого своего хода, оканчивая заклинание на себе при успехе.
Урон увеличивается на {@dice 1d6} за каждый уровень ячейки заклинания выше 2.
Chorus of the Lost.name: Хор потерянных
Chromatic Orb.entries.0: Вы метаете шар энергии в цель в пределах дистанции. Выберите тип создаваемого шара — кислота, холод, огонь, электричество, яд или звук — а затем совершите дальнобойную атаку заклинанием по цели. При попадании цель получает {@damage 3d8} урона выбранного типа.
Chromatic Orb.entries.1: Если на двух или более d8 выпадет одинаковое число, шар перескакивает на другую цель на ваш выбор в пределах 30 футов от цели. Совершите бросок атаки по новой цели и новый бросок урона. Шар не может перескочить снова, если вы не накладываете заклинание с помощью ячейки 2-го уровня или выше.
Chromatic Orb.entriesHigherLevel.0.entri: Урон увеличивается на {@scaledamage 3d8|1-9|1d8} за каждый уровень ячейки заклинания выше 1. Шар может перескочить максимальное число раз, равное уровню потраченной ячейки, и каждая цель может быть выбрана только один раз при каждом накладывании этого заклинания.
Chromatic Orb.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Chromatic Orb.name: Цветной шар
Circle of Death.components.m: порошок измельчённой чёрной жемчужины стоимостью 500+ зм
Circle of Death.entries.0: Негативная энергия расходится {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Сферой} с радиусом 60 футов из точки, выбранной вами в пределах дистанции. Каждое существо в этой области совершает спасбросок Телосложения, получая {@damage 8d8} урона некротической энергией при провале или половину этого урона при успехе.
Circle of Death.entriesHigherLevel.0.ent: Урон увеличивается на {@scaledamage 8d8|6-9|2d8} за каждый уровень ячейки заклинания выше 6.
Circle of Death.name: Круг смерти
Circle of Power.entries.0: Аура исходит от вас 30-футовой {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Эманацией} на время действия заклинания. Находясь в ауре, вы и ваши союзники совершаете с {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB|Преимуществом} спасброски против заклинаний и других магических эффектов. Когда подвергшееся действию существо совершает спасбросок против заклинания или магического эффекта, позволяющего при спасброске получить лишь половину урона, оно не получает урона вовсе при успешном спасброске.
Circle of Power.name: Круг силы
Circle of Scarlet.components.m: клочок пергамента с кругом, нарисованным кровью гуманоида
Circle of Scarlet.entries.0: В рамках накладывания этого заклинания вы должны потратить четыре {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Кости Хитов}, иначе заклинание автоматически проваливается. Багровый столб вырывается из земли в {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Цилиндре} с радиусом 20 футов и высотой 100 футов с центром в точке в пределах дистанции. Каждое существо в пределах {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Цилиндра} совершает спасбросок Телосложения.
Circle of Scarlet.entries.1: При провале существо получает урон некротической энергией, равный броску {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Костей Хитов}, потраченных на заклинание, плюс модификатор вашей заклинательной характеристики. При успехе существо получает половину этого урона.
Circle of Scarlet.entries.2: За каждое существо, провалившее спасбросок против этого заклинания, вы получаете 10 {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB|временных хитов}.
Circle of Scarlet.name: Алый круг
Clairvoyance.components.m: фокусировка стоимостью 100+ зм — либо украшенный драгоценными камнями рог для слуха, либо стеклянный глаз для зрения
Clairvoyance.entries.0: Вы создаёте {@condition Invisible|XPHB|Невидимый} сенсор в пределах дистанции в знакомом вам месте (там, где вы бывали или которое видели) или в очевидном месте, которое вам незнакомо (например, за дверью, за углом или в роще деревьев). Неосязаемый, неуязвимый сенсор остаётся на месте на время действия заклинания.
Clairvoyance.entries.1: Когда вы накладываете заклинание, выберите зрение или слух. Вы можете использовать выбранное чувство через сенсор, как если бы находились в его пространстве. {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB|Бонусным действием} вы можете переключаться между зрением и слухом.
Clairvoyance.entries.2: Существо, которое видит сенсор (например, существо под действием {@spell See Invisibility|XPHB|Видение невидимого} или {@sense Truesight|XPHB|Истинного зрения}), видит светящийся шар размером примерно с ваш кулак.
Clairvoyance.name: Ясновидение
Clone.components.m: алмаз стоимостью 1 000+ зм, который расходуется заклинанием, и герметичный сосуд стоимостью 2 000+ зм, достаточно большой, чтобы вместить клонируемое существо
Clone.entries.0: Вы касаетесь существа или по меньшей мере 1 кубического дюйма его плоти. Инертная копия этого существа формируется внутри сосуда, использованного при накладывании заклинания, и завершает рост через 120 дней; вы выбираете, будет ли готовый клон того же возраста, что и существо, или моложе. Клон остаётся инертным и сохраняется бесконечно долго, пока его сосуд остаётся нетронутым.
Clone.entries.1: Если оригинальное существо умирает после того, как клон завершит формирование, душа существа переходит в клон, если душа свободна и желает вернуться. Клон физически идентичен оригиналу и обладает той же личностью, воспоминаниями и способностями, но не имеет снаряжения оригинала. Изначальные останки существа, если таковые имеются, становятся инертными и не могут быть оживлены, поскольку душа существа находится в другом месте.
Clone.name: Клон
Cloud of Daggers.entries.0: Вы создаёте вращающиеся кинжалы в {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Кубе} 5 футов с центром в точке в пределах дистанции. Каждое существо в этой области получает {@damage 4d4} рубящего урона. Существо также получает этот урон, если входит в {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Куб}, заканчивает там свой ход или если {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Куб} перемещается в его пространство. Существо получает этот урон только один раз за ход.
Cloud of Daggers.entries.1: В свои последующие ходы вы можете действием {@action Magic|XPHB|Магия} телепортировать {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Куб} на расстояние до 30 футов.
Cloud of Daggers.entriesHigherLevel.0.en: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 4d4|2-9|2d4} for each spell slot level above 2.
Cloud of Daggers.name: Cloud of Daggers
Cloudkill.entries.0: You create a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} of yellow-green fog centered on a point within range. The fog lasts for the duration or until strong wind (such as the one created by {@spell Gust of Wind|XPHB}) disperses it, ending the spell. Its area is {@variantrule Heavily Obscured|XPHB}.
Cloudkill.entries.1: Each creature in the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} makes a Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 5d8} Poison damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature must also make this save when the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} moves into its space and when it enters the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} or ends its turn there. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
Cloudkill.entries.2: The {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} moves 10 feet away from you at the start of each of your turns.
Cloudkill.entriesHigherLevel.0.entries.0: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 5d8|5-9|1d8} for each spell slot level above 5.
Cloudkill.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Cloudkill.name: Cloudkill
Color Spray.entries.0: You launch a dazzling array of flashing, colorful light. Each creature in a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} originating from you must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or have the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition until the end of your next turn.
Color Spray.name: Color Spray
Command.entries.0: You speak a one-word command to a creature you can see within range. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or follow the command on its next turn. Choose the command from these options:
Command.entries.1.items.0.entries.0: The target moves toward you by the shortest and most direct route, ending its turn if it moves within 5 feet of you.
Command.entries.1.items.0.name: Approach
Command.entries.1.items.1.entries.0: The target drops whatever it is holding and then ends its turn.
Command.entries.1.items.1.name: Drop
Command.entries.1.items.2.entries.0: The target spends its turn moving away from you by the fastest available means.
Command.entries.1.items.2.name: Flee
Command.entries.1.items.3.entries.0: The target has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and then ends its turn.
Command.entries.1.items.3.name: Grovel
Command.entries.1.items.4.entries.0: On its turn, the target doesn't move and takes no action or {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Command.entries.1.items.4.name: Halt
Command.entriesHigherLevel.0.entries.0: You can affect one additional creature for each spell slot level above 1.
Command.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Command.name: Command
Commune with Nature.entries.0: You commune with nature spirits and gain knowledge of the surrounding area. In the outdoors, the spell gives you knowledge of the area within 3 miles of you. In caves and other natural underground settings, the radius is limited to 300 feet. The spell doesn't function where nature has been replaced by construction, such as in castles and settlements.
Commune with Nature.entries.1: Choose three of the following facts; you learn those facts as they pertain to the spell's area:
Commune with Nature.entries.2.items.1: Locations of portals to other planes of existence
Commune with Nature.entries.2.items.2: Location of one {@variantrule Challenge Rating|XPHB} 10+ creature (DM's choice) that is a Celestial, an Elemental, a Fey, a Fiend, or an Undead
Commune with Nature.entries.2.items.3: The most prevalent kind of plant, mineral, or Beast (you choose which to learn)
Commune with Nature.entries.3: For example, you could determine the location of a powerful monster in the area, the locations of bodies of water, and the locations of any towns.
Commune with Nature.name: Commune with Nature
Commune.entries.0: You contact a deity or a divine proxy and ask up to three questions that can be answered with yes or no. You must ask your questions before the spell ends. You receive a correct answer for each question.
Commune.entries.1: Divine beings aren't necessarily omniscient, so you might receive "unclear" as an answer if a question pertains to information that lies beyond the deity's knowledge. In a case where a one-word answer could be misleading or contrary to the deity's interests, the DM might offer a short phrase as an answer instead.
Commune.entries.2: If you cast the spell more than once before finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, there is a cumulative {@chance 25|||No answer!|Answer} chance for each casting after the first that you get no answer.
Commune.name: Commune
Compelled Duel.entries.0: You try to compel a creature into a duel. One creature that you can see within range makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against creatures other than you, and it can't willingly move to a space that is more than 30 feet away from you.
Compelled Duel.entries.1: The spell ends if you make an attack roll against a creature other than the target, if you cast a spell on an enemy other than the target, if an ally of yours damages the target, or if you end your turn more than 30 feet away from the target.
Compelled Duel.name: Compelled Duel
Comprehend Languages.components.m: a pinch of soot and salt
Comprehend Languages.entries.0: For the duration, you understand the literal meaning of any language that you hear or see signed. You also understand any written language that you see, but you must be touching the surface on which the words are written. It takes about 1 minute to read one page of text. This spell doesn't decode symbols or secret messages.
Comprehend Languages.name: Comprehend Languages
Compulsion.entries.0: Each creature of your choice that you can see within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition until the spell ends.
Compulsion.entries.1: For the duration, you can take a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to designate a direction that is horizontal to you. Each {@condition Charmed|XPHB} target must use as much of its movement as possible to move in that direction on its next turn, taking the safest route. After moving in this way, a target repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.
Compulsion.name: Compulsion
Concealed Shot.entries.0: A Ranged weapon you touch is made supernaturally subtle. For the duration, when you make a ranged attack using the weapon, the weapon or ammunition you're using becomes {@condition invisible} while in flight and the weapon becomes silent. If the weapon produces smoke or light, the spell suppresses these effects. The weapon or projectile you're using becomes visible again after the attack hits or misses. If you are hidden and the target is 80 feet or further from you, the attack doesn't reveal your location.
Concealed Shot.name: Concealed Shot
Cone of Cold.components.m: a small crystal or glass cone
Cone of Cold.entries.0: You unleash a blast of cold air. Each creature in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} originating from you makes a Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 8d8} Cold damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature killed by this spell becomes a frozen statue until it thaws.
Cone of Cold.entriesHigherLevel.0.entrie: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 8d8|5-9|1d8} for each spell slot level above 5.
Cone of Cold.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Cone of Cold.name: Cone of Cold
Confusion.entries.0: Each creature in a 10-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on a point you choose within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw, or that target can't take Bonus Actions or Reactions and must roll {@dice 1d10} at the start of each of its turns to determine its behavior for that turn, consulting the table below.
Confusion.entries.1.rows.0.1: The target doesn't take an action, and it uses all its movement to move. Roll {@dice 1d4} for the direction: {@b 1}, north; {@b 2}, east; {@b 3}, south; or {@b 4}, west.
Confusion.entries.1.rows.1.1: The target doesn't move or take actions.
Confusion.entries.1.rows.2.1: The target doesn't move, and it takes the {@action Attack|XPHB} action to make one melee attack against a random creature within reach. If none are within reach, the target takes no action.
Confusion.entries.2: At the end of each of its turns, an affected target repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.
Confusion.entriesHigherLevel.0.entries.0: The {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}'s radius increases by 5 feet for each spell slot level above 4.
Confusion.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Confusion.name: Confusion
Conjure Animals.entries.0: You conjure nature spirits that appear as a Large pack of spectral, intangible animals in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The pack lasts for the duration, and you choose the spirits' animal form, such as wolves, serpents, or birds.
Conjure Animals.entries.1: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength saving throws while you're within 5 feet of the pack, and when you move on your turn, you can also move the pack up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.
Conjure Animals.entries.2: Whenever the pack moves within 10 feet of a creature you can see and whenever a creature you can see enters a space within 10 feet of the pack or ends its turn there, you can force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 3d10} Slashing damage. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
Conjure Animals.entriesHigherLevel.0.ent: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 3d10|3-9|1d10} for each spell slot level above 3.
Conjure Animals.name: Conjure Animals
Conjure Barrage.components.m: a Melee or Ranged weapon worth at least 1 CP
Conjure Barrage.entries.0: You brandish the weapon used to cast the spell and conjure similar spectral weapons (or ammunition appropriate to the weapon) that launch forward and then disappear. Each creature of your choice that you can see in a 60-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 5d8} Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
Conjure Barrage.entriesHigherLevel.0.ent: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 5d8|3-9|1d8} for each spell slot level above 3.
Conjure Barrage.name: Conjure Barrage
Conjure Cannonball.entries.0: You summon a cannonball, mid-flight and at full velocity, which explodes on impact. Make a ranged spell attack roll against a target you can see within range. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 5d10} Bludgeoning damage and an explosion extends from it in a 5-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. Each creature other than the target within the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking half as much damage as the target on a failed save.
The damage increases by {@dice 1d10} for each slot level above 3.
Conjure Cannonball.name: Conjure Cannonball
Conjure Celestial.entries.0: You conjure a spirit from the Upper Planes, which manifests as a pillar of light in a 10-foot-radius, 40-foot-high {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on a point within range. For each creature you can see in the {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder}, choose which of these lights shines on it:
Conjure Celestial.entries.1.items.0.entr: The target regains {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 4d12} plus your spellcasting ability modifier.
Conjure Celestial.entries.1.items.0.name: Healing Light
Conjure Celestial.entries.1.items.1.entr: The target makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 6d12} Radiant damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
Conjure Celestial.entries.1.items.1.name: Searing Light
Conjure Celestial.entries.2: Until the spell ends, {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} fills the {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder}, and when you move on your turn, you can also move the {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} up to 30 feet.
Conjure Celestial.entries.3: Whenever the {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} moves into the space of a creature you can see and whenever a creature you can see enters the {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} or ends its turn there, you can bathe it in one of the lights. A creature can be affected by this spell only once per turn.
Conjure Celestial.entriesHigherLevel.0.e: The healing and damage increase by {@scaledamage 6d12|7-9|1d12} for each spell slot level above 7.
Conjure Celestial.name: Conjure Celestial
Conjure Constructs.entries.0: You conjure a group of intangible, orderly spirits that appear as a Medium group of modrons or other Constructs in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The spirits last for the duration. When you cast this spell and as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on subsequent turns, you can command the spirits to target one creature or object you can see within 5 feet of the spirits and create one of the following effects:
Conjure Constructs.entries.1.items.0.ent: The target makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d6} Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
Conjure Constructs.entries.1.items.1.ent: The target gains {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 1d6} plus your spellcasting ability modifier.
Conjure Constructs.entries.2: When you move on your turn, you can also move the spirits up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.
The damage and {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} both increase by {@scaledamage 3d6|3-9|1d6} for each spell slot level above 3.
Conjure Constructs.name: Conjure Constructs
Conjure Cover.entries.0: You conjure a low cobblestone wall along the ground at a point you can see within range. The wall is 18 inches thick and is composed of three 5-foot-long, 3-foot-high segments. Each segment must be contiguous with at least one other segment.
Conjure Cover.entries.1: A Medium creature that hunkers behind the wall has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Half Cover}, and a Small creature that hunkers behind it has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Three-Quarters Cover}. The wall can be leapt over without spending any additional movement.
Conjure Cover.entries.2: Each segment has AC 10 and 30 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}. Reducing a segment of the wall to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} causes it to crumble, destroying it. The wall disappears when all the segments are destroyed or the spell ends.
Conjure Cover.name: Conjure Cover
Conjure Elemental.entries.0: You conjure a Large, intangible spirit from the Elemental Planes that appears in an unoccupied space within range. Choose the spirit's element, which determines its damage type: air (Lightning), earth (Thunder), fire (Fire), or water (Cold). The spirit lasts for the duration.
Conjure Elemental.entries.1: Whenever a creature you can see enters the spirit's space or starts its turn within 5 feet of the spirit, you can force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw if the spirit has no creature {@condition Restrained|XPHB}. On failed save, the target takes {@damage 8d8} damage of the spirit's type, and the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the spell ends. At the start of each of its turns, the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} target repeats the save. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 4d8} damage of the spirit's type. On a successful save, the target isn't {@condition Restrained|XPHB} by the spirit.
Conjure Elemental.entriesHigherLevel.0.e: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 8d8;4d8|5-9|1d8} for each spell slot level above 5.
Conjure Elemental.name: Conjure Elemental
Conjure Fey.entries.0: You conjure a Medium spirit from the Feywild in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The spirit lasts for the duration, and it looks like a Fey creature of your choice. When the spirit appears, you can make one melee spell attack against a creature within 5 feet of it. On a hit, the target takes Psychic damage equal to {@damage 3d12} plus your spellcasting ability modifier, and the target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn, with both you and the spirit as the source of the fear.
Conjure Fey.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on your later turns, you can teleport the spirit to an unoccupied space you can see within 30 feet of the space it left and make the attack against a creature within 5 feet of it.
Conjure Fey.entriesHigherLevel.0.entries: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 3d12|6-9|1d12} for each spell slot level above 6.
Conjure Fey.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Conjure Fey.name: Conjure Fey
Conjure Minor Elementals.entries.0: You conjure spirits from the Elemental Planes that flit around you in a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} for the duration. Until the spell ends, any attack you make deals an extra {@damage 2d8} damage when you hit a creature in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. This damage is Acid, Cold, Fire, or Lightning (your choice when you make the attack).
Conjure Minor Elementals.entries.1: In addition, the ground in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} is {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB} for your enemies.
Conjure Minor Elementals.entriesHigherLe: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 2d8|4-9|1d8} for each spell slot level above 4.
Conjure Minor Elementals.name: Conjure Minor Elementals
Conjure Plants.entries.0: You conjure animated plants that appear as a Large mass of vines, stems, shoots, roots, and leaves in an unoccupied space you can see within range. The vegetation lasts for the duration.
Conjure Plants.entries.1: While you're within 5 feet of the plants or sharing a space with them, you are considered to be in a {@variantrule Lightly Obscured|XPHB} area. Other creatures cannot enter the same space as the plants. When you move on your turn, you can also move the plants up to 30 feet to an unoccupied space you can see.
Conjure Plants.entries.2: Whenever the plants move within 5 feet of a creature you can see, and whenever a creature you can see enters a space within 5 feet of the plants or ends its turn there, you can force that creature to make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 3d6} Slashing damage. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
Conjure Plants.entriesHigherLevel.0.entr: The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 3.
Conjure Plants.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Conjure Plants.name: Conjure Plants
Conjure Volley.components.m: a Melee or Ranged weapon worth at least 1 CP
Conjure Volley.entries.0: You brandish the weapon used to cast the spell and choose a point within range. Hundreds of similar spectral weapons (or ammunition appropriate to the weapon) fall in a volley and then disappear. Each creature of your choice that you can see in a 40-foot-radius, 20-foot-high {@variantrule Cylinder [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cylinder} centered on that point makes a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes {@damage 8d8} Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
Conjure Volley.name: Conjure Volley
Conjure Woodland Beings.entries.0: You conjure nature spirits that flit around you in a 10-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} for the duration. Whenever the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} enters the space of a creature you can see and whenever a creature you can see enters the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} or ends its turn there, you can force that creature to make a Wisdom saving throw. The creature takes {@damage 5d8} Force damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
Conjure Woodland Beings.entries.1: In addition, you can take the {@action Disengage|XPHB} action as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} for the spell's duration.
Conjure Woodland Beings.entriesHigherLev: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 5d8|4-9|1d8} for each spell slot level above 4.
Conjure Woodland Beings.name: Conjure Woodland Beings
Consume Mind.components.m: a 1-ounce fresh or magically preserved portion of another creature's brain
Consume Mind.entries.0: You consume the brain of a dead creature, gaining access to its memories. The creature must have a brain and can't be Undead. The spell fails if the creature has been dead (and not preserved) for more than 3 days.
Consume Mind.entries.1: Until the spell ends, you can attempt to recall a memory, such as family history, recent events, building layouts, passwords, details of the creature's death, and similar information. To recall a memory, you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action and make an ability check using your spellcasting ability modifier. The DC equals the corpse's Intelligence ability score.
Consume Mind.entries.2: Once you take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to recall information, you can't attempt to recall that specific piece of information again.
Consume Mind.name: Consume Mind
Consuming Pyre.entries.0: You cause a creature that you can see within range to burst into consuming, relentless flames. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 2d8} Fire damage and {@damage 2d8} Necrotic or Radiant damage (choose when you cast the spell), and the radiant flames linger. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only.
Consuming Pyre.entries.1: While the flames linger, the creature takes the damage again at the start of each of its turns. The target, or another creature within reach of the target, can take an action to allow the target to repeat the saving throw, ending the spell on a success.
Consuming Pyre.entries.2: A creature killed by the damage from this spell is reduced to ash, along with any nonmagical equipment it is wearing or carrying.
Consuming Pyre.name: Consuming Pyre
Consumption.entries.0: As part of casting this spell, you must expend one {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} or the spell automatically fails. Choose a creature within range and roll the expended {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die}. The target takes Necrotic damage equal to the result. At the end of each of its turns while the spell lasts, the target makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes this damage again. On a successful save, the spell ends.
Consumption.entriesHigherLevel.0.entries: Вы можете выбрать одно дополнительное существо за каждый уровень ячейки заклинания выше 1-го.
Consumption.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Consumption.name: Consumption
Contact Other Plane.entries.0: You mentally contact a demigod, the spirit of a long-dead sage, or some other knowledgeable entity from another plane. Contacting this otherworldly intelligence can break your mind. When you cast this spell, make a {@dc 15} Intelligence saving throw. On a successful save, you can ask the entity up to five questions. You must ask your questions before the spell ends. The DM answers each question with one word, such as "yes," "no," "maybe," "never," "irrelevant," or "unclear" (if the entity doesn't know the answer to the question). If a one-word answer would be misleading, the DM might instead offer a short phrase as an answer.
Contact Other Plane.entries.1: On a failed save, you take {@damage 6d6} Psychic damage and have the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition until you finish a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}. A {@spell Greater Restoration|XPHB} spell cast on you ends this effect.
Contact Other Plane.name: Contact Other Plane
Contagion.entries.0: Your touch inflicts a magical contagion. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or take {@damage 11d8} Necrotic damage and have the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition. Also, choose one ability when you cast the spell. While {@condition Poisoned|XPHB}, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on saving throws made with the chosen ability.
Contagion.entries.1: The target must repeat the saving throw at the end of each of its turns until it gets three successes or failures. If the target succeeds on three of these saves, the spell ends on the target. If the target fails three of the saves, the spell lasts for 7 days on it.
Contagion.entries.2: Whenever the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} target receives an effect that would end the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition, the target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw, or the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition doesn't end on it.
Contagion.name: Contagion
Contingency.components.m: a gem-encrusted statuette of yourself worth 1,500+ GP
Contingency.entries.0: Choose a spell of level 5 or lower that you can cast, that has a casting time of an action, and that can target you. You cast that spell—called the contingent spell—as part of casting Contingency, expending spell slots for both, but the contingent spell doesn't come into effect. Instead, it takes effect when a certain trigger occurs. You describe that trigger when you cast the two spells. For example, a Contingency cast with {@spell Water Breathing|XPHB} might stipulate that Water Breathing comes into effect when you are engulfed in water or a similar liquid.
Contingency.entries.1: The contingent spell takes effect immediately after the trigger occurs for the first time, whether or not you want it to, and then Contingency ends.
Contingency.entries.2: The contingent spell takes effect only on you, even if it can normally target others. You can use only one Contingency spell at a time. If you cast this spell again, the effect of another Contingency spell on you ends. Also, Contingency ends on you if its material component is ever not on your person.
Contingency.name: Contingency
Continual Flame.components.m: ruby dust worth 50+ GP, which the spell consumes
Continual Flame.entries.0: A flame springs from an object that you touch. The effect casts {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 20-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 20 feet. It looks like a regular flame, but it creates no heat and consumes no fuel. The flame can be covered or hidden but not smothered or quenched.
Continual Flame.name: Continual Flame
Control Flames.entries.0: You choose nonmagical flame that you can see within range and that fits within a 5-foot cube. You affect it in one of the following ways:
Control Flames.entries.1.items.0: You instantaneously expand the flame 5 feet in one direction, provided that wood or other fuel is present in the new location.
Control Flames.entries.1.items.1: You instantaneously extinguish the flames within the cube.
Control Flames.entries.1.items.2: You double or halve the area of bright light and dim light cast by the flame, change its color, or both. The change lasts for 1 hour.
Control Flames.entries.1.items.3: You cause simple shapes—such as the vague form of a creature, an inanimate object, or a location—to appear within the flames and animate as you like. The shapes last for 1 hour.
Control Flames.entries.2: If you cast this spell multiple times, you can have up to three non-instantaneous effects created by it active at a time, and you can dismiss such an effect as an action.
Control Flames.name: Control Flames
Control Water.components.m: a mixture of water and dust
Control Water.entries.0: Until the spell ends, you control any water inside an area you choose that is a {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} up to 100 feet on a side, using one of the following effects. As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your later turns, you can repeat the same effect or choose a different one.
Control Water.entries.1.entries.0: You cause the water level of all standing water in the area to rise by as much as 20 feet. If you choose an area in a large body of water, you instead create a 20-foot tall wave that travels from one side of the area to the other and then crashes. Any Huge or smaller vehicles in the wave's path are carried with it to the other side. Any Huge or smaller vehicles struck by the wave have a {@chance 25|||Capsizes!|No effect} chance of capsizing.
Control Water.entries.1.entries.1: The water level remains elevated until the spell ends or you choose a different effect. If this effect produced a wave, the wave repeats on the start of your next turn while the flood effect lasts.
Control Water.entries.1.name: Flood
Control Water.entries.2.entries.0: You part water in the area and create a trench. The trench extends across the spell's area, and the separated water forms a wall to either side. The trench remains until the spell ends or you choose a different effect. The water then slowly fills in the trench over the course of the next round until the normal water level is restored.
Control Water.entries.2.name: Part Water
Control Water.entries.3.entries.0: You cause flowing water in the area to move in a direction you choose, even if the water has to flow over obstacles, up walls, or in other unlikely directions. The water in the area moves as you direct it, but once it moves beyond the spell's area, it resumes its flow based on the terrain. The water continues to move in the direction you chose until the spell ends or you choose a different effect.
Control Water.entries.3.name: Redirect Flow
Control Water.entries.4.entries.0: You cause a whirlpool to form in the center of the area, which must be at least 50 feet square and 25 feet deep. The whirlpool lasts until you choose a different effect or the spell ends. The whirlpool is 5 feet wide at the base, up to 50 feet wide at the top, and 25 feet tall. Any creature in the water and within 25 feet of the whirlpool is pulled 10 feet toward it. When a creature enters the whirlpool for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, it makes a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 2d8} Bludgeoning damage. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage. A creature can swim away from the whirlpool only if it first takes an action to pull away and succeeds on a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against your spell save DC.
Control Water.entries.4.name: Whirlpool
Control Water.name: Control Water
Control Weather.entries.0: You take control of the weather within 5 miles of you for the duration. You must be outdoors to cast this spell, and it ends early if you go indoors.
Control Weather.entries.1: When you cast the spell, you change the current weather conditions, which are determined by the DM. You can change precipitation, temperature, and wind. It takes {@dice 1d4 × 10} minutes for the new conditions to take effect. Once they do so, you can change the conditions again. When the spell ends, the weather gradually returns to normal.
Control Weather.entries.2: When you change the weather conditions, find a current condition on the following tables and change its stage by one, up or down. When changing the wind, you can change its direction.
Control Weather.entries.3.caption: Precipitation
Control Weather.entries.3.rows.4.1: Torrential rain, driving hail, or blizzard
Control Weather.entries.4.caption: Temperature
Control Weather.entries.5.caption: Wind
Control Weather.name: Control Weather
Control Winds.entries.0: You take control of the air in a 100-foot cube that you can see within range. Choose one of the following effects when you cast the spell. The effect lasts for the spell's duration, unless you use your action on a later turn to switch to a different effect. You can also use your action to temporarily halt the effect or to restart one you've halted.
Control Winds.entries.1.entries.0: A wind picks up within the cube, continually blowing in a horizontal direction you designate. You choose the intensity of the wind: calm, moderate, or strong. If the wind is moderate or strong, ranged weapon attacks that pass through it or that are made against targets within the cube have disadvantage on their attack rolls. If the wind is strong, any creature moving against the wind must spend 1 extra foot of movement for each foot moved.
Control Winds.entries.1.name: Gusts
Control Winds.entries.2.entries.0: You cause a sustained blast of strong wind to blow downward from the top of the cube. Ranged weapon attacks that pass through the cube or that are made against targets within it have disadvantage on their attack rolls. A creature must make a Strength saving throw if it flies into the cube for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there flying. On a failed save, the creature is knocked {@condition prone}.
Control Winds.entries.2.name: Downdraft
Control Winds.entries.3.entries.0: You cause a sustained updraft within the cube, rising upward from the cube's bottom side. Creatures that end a fall within the cube take only half damage from the fall. When a creature in the cube makes a vertical jump, the creature can jump up to 10 feet higher than normal.
Control Winds.entries.3.name: Updraft
Control Winds.name: Control Winds
Cordon of Arrows.components.m: four or more arrows or bolts
Cordon of Arrows.entries.0: You touch up to four nonmagical Arrows or Bolts and plant them in the ground in your space. Until the spell ends, the ammunition can't be physically uprooted, and whenever a creature other than you enters a space within 30 feet of the ammunition for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, one piece of ammunition flies up to strike it. The creature must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 2d4} Piercing damage. The piece of ammunition is then destroyed. The spell ends when none of the ammunition remains planted in the ground.
Cordon of Arrows.entries.1: When you cast this spell, you can designate any creatures you choose, and the spell ignores them.
Cordon of Arrows.entriesHigherLevel.0.en: The amount of ammunition that can be affected increases by two for each spell slot level above 2.
Cordon of Arrows.name: Cordon of Arrows
Counterspell.entries.0: You attempt to interrupt a creature in the process of casting a spell. The creature makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the spell dissipates with no effect, and the action, {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, or {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} used to cast it is wasted. If that spell was cast with a spell slot, the slot isn't expended.
Counterspell.name: Counterspell
Create Bonfire.entries.0: You create a bonfire on ground that you can see within range. Until the spell ends, the magic bonfire fills a 5-foot cube. Any creature in the bonfire's space when you cast the spell must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or take {@damage 1d8} fire damage. A creature must also make the saving throw when it moves into the bonfire's space for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there.
Create Bonfire.entries.1: The bonfire ignites flammable objects in its area that aren't being worn or carried.
Create Bonfire.entries.2: The spell's damage increases by {@damage 1d8} when you reach 5th level ({@damage 2d8}), 11th level ({@damage 3d8}), and 17th level ({@damage 4d8}).
Create Bonfire.name: Create Bonfire
Create Food and Water.entries.0: You create 45 pounds of food and 30 gallons of fresh water on the ground or in containers within range—both useful in fending off the hazards of malnutrition and dehydration. The food is bland but nourishing and looks like a food of your choice, and the water is clean. The food spoils after 24 hours if uneaten.
Create Food and Water.name: Create Food and Water
Create Homunculus.components.m: clay, ash, and mandrake root, all of which the spell consumes, and a jewel-encrusted dagger worth at least 1,000 gp
Create Homunculus.entries.0: While speaking an intricate incantation, you cut yourself with a jewel-encrusted dagger, taking {@damage 2d4} piercing damage that can't be reduced in any way. You then drip your blood on the spell's other components and touch them, transforming them into a special construct called a {@creature homunculus}.
Create Homunculus.entries.1: The statistics of the {@creature homunculus} are in the Monster Manual. It is your faithful companion, and it dies if you die. Whenever you finish a long rest, you can spend up to half your Hit Dice if the homunculus is on the same plane of existence as you. When you do so, roll each die and add your Constitution modifier to it. Your hit point maximum is reduced by the total, and the homunculus's hit point maximum and current hit points are both increased by it. This process can reduce you to no lower than 1 hit point, and the change to your and the homunculus's hit points ends when you finish your next long rest. The reduction to your hit point maximum can't be removed by any means before then, except by the homunculus's death.
Create Homunculus.entries.2: You can have only one homunculus at a time. If you cast this spell while your homunculus lives, the spell fails.
Create Homunculus.name: Create Homunculus
Create Magen.components.m: a vial of quicksilver worth 500 gp and a life-sized human doll, both of which the spell consumes, and an intricate crystal rod worth at least 1,500 gp that is not consumed
Create Magen.entries.0: While casting the spell, you place a vial of quicksilver in the chest of a life-sized human doll stuffed with ash or dust. You then stitch up the doll and drip your blood on it. At the end of the casting, you tap the doll with a crystal rod, transforming it into a {@filter magen|bestiary|search=magen|source=IDRotF} clothed in whatever the doll was wearing. The type of magen is chosen by you during the casting of the spell. See {@adventure appendix C|IDRotF|21} for different kinds of magen and their statistics.
Create Magen.entries.1: When the magen appears, your hit point maximum decreases by an amount equal to the magen's challenge rating (minimum reduction of 1). Only a {@spell wish} spell can undo this reduction to your hit point maximum.
Create Magen.entries.2: Any magen you create with this spell obeys your commands without question.
Create Magen.name: Create Magen
Create Spelljamming Helm.components.m: a crystal rod worth at least 5,000 gp, which the spell consumes
Create Spelljamming Helm.entries.0: Holding the rod used in the casting of the spell, you touch a Large or smaller chair that is unoccupied. The rod disappears, and the chair is transformed into a {@item spelljamming helm|AAG}.
Create Spelljamming Helm.name: Create Spelljamming Helm
Create Undead.components.m: one 150+ GP black onyx stone for each corpse
Create Undead.entries.0: You can cast this spell only at night. Choose up to three corpses of Medium or Small Humanoids within range. Each one becomes a {@creature Ghoul|XMM} under your control (see the {@book Monster Manual|XMM} for its stat block).
Create Undead.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on each of your turns, you can mentally command any creature you animated with this spell if the creature is within 120 feet of you (if you control multiple creatures, you can command any of them at the same time, issuing the same command to them). You decide what action the creature will take and where it will move on its next turn, or you can issue a general command, such as to guard a particular place. If you issue no commands, the creature takes the {@action Dodge|XPHB} action and moves only to avoid harm. Once given an order, the creature continues to follow the order until its task is complete.
Create Undead.entries.2: The creature is under your control for 24 hours, after which it stops obeying any command you've given it. To maintain control of the creature for another 24 hours, you must cast this spell on the creature before the current 24-hour period ends. This use of the spell reasserts your control over up to three creatures you have animated with this spell rather than animating new ones.
Create Undead.entriesHigherLevel.0.entri: If you use a level 7 spell slot, you can animate or reassert control over four {@creature Ghoul|XMM|Ghouls}. If you use a level 8 spell slot, you can animate or reassert control over five {@creature Ghoul|XMM|Ghouls} or two {@creature Ghast|XMM|Ghasts} or {@creature Wight|XMM|Wights}. If you use a level 9 spell slot, you can animate or reassert control over six {@creature Ghoul|XMM|Ghouls}, three {@creature Ghast|XMM|Ghasts} or {@creature Wight|XMM|Wights}, or two {@creature Mummy|XMM|Mummies}. See the {@book Monster Manual|XMM} for these stat blocks.
Create Undead.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Create Undead.name: Create Undead
Create or Destroy Water.components.m: a mix of water and sand
Create or Destroy Water.entries.0: You do one of the following:
You create up to 10 gallons of clean water within range in an open container. Alternatively, the water falls as rain in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} within range, extinguishing exposed flames there.
You destroy up to 10 gallons of water in an open container within range. Alternatively, you destroy fog in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} within range.
Create or Destroy Water.entriesHigherLev: You create or destroy 10 additional gallons of water, or the size of the {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} increases by 5 feet, for each spell slot level above 1.
Create or Destroy Water.name: Create or Destroy Water
Creation.entries.0: You pull wisps of shadow material from the Shadowfell to create an object within range. It is either an object of vegetable matter (soft goods, rope, wood, and the like) or mineral matter (stone, crystal, metal, and the like). The object must be no larger than a 5-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}, and the object must be of a form and material that you have seen.
Creation.entries.1: The spell's duration depends on the object's material, as shown in the Materials table. If the object is composed of multiple materials, use the shortest duration. Using any object created by this spell as another spell's Material component causes the other spell to fail.
Creation.entries.2.caption: Materials
Creation.entriesHigherLevel.0.entries.0: The {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} increases by 5 feet for each spell slot level above 5.
Creation.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Creation.name: Создание
Creeping Death.entries.0: As part of casting this spell, you must expend eight {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} or the spell automatically fails. Roll the expended {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}. The target's Creeping Death threshold is equal to the roll's total. For the spell's duration, if the creature's {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} are reduced to or lower than its Creeping Death threshold, the creature immediately dies.
Creeping Death.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can force the target to make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, roll {@dice 2d6} and add the result to the creature's Creeping Death threshold. On a successful save, add half the result instead. If the creature succeeds on three saving throws, which don't have to be consecutive, this spell ends early.
Creeping Death.name: Creeping Death
Creeping Rot.entries.0: You send tendrils of black and gray rot toward a target that you can see within range. Three tendrils then leap from that target to as many as three other targets of your choice, each of which must be within 30 feet of the first target. A target can be a creature or a nonmagical plant that isn't a creature, such as a tree or shrub. In either case, each can be targeted by only one of the tendrils.
Creeping Rot.entries.1: A target that is a creature must make a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, it takes {@damage 3d8} Necrotic damage and has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for the duration. On a successful save, it takes half as much damage only. A creature {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} in this way takes {@damage 1d8} Necrotic damage at the start of each of its turns. A {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} creature repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the spell on itself on a success.
Creeping Rot.entries.2: A Plant creature automatically fails the initial save, and a nonmagical plant that isn't a creature simply withers and dies.
Creeping Rot.entriesHigherLevel.0.entrie: One additional tendril leaps from the first target to another target for each spell slot level above 3.
Creeping Rot.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Creeping Rot.name: Creeping Rot
Creeping Touch.entries.0: You detach your hand at the wrist, transforming it into a {@creature Spider|XMM}. While the spider is within 100 feet of you, you can communicate with it telepathically. Additionally, as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you can see through its eyes and hear what it hears until the start of your next turn. During this time, you have no awareness of your own surroundings.
Creeping Touch.entries.1: If the spider is killed or doesn't return to you before the spell ends, your hand is restored, but you take {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can command the spider to return to you. Once it arrives, it reattaches as your hand and the spell ends.
Creeping Touch.name: Creeping Touch
Crimson Harvest.entries.0: A wave of blood sweeps out from you in a 15-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. Each creature of your choice in the wave must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d10} Necrotic damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
Crimson Harvest.entries.1: If you damage at least one Small or larger creature, one creature of your choice that you can see within the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the damage roll.
Crimson Harvest.entriesHigherLevel.0.ent: The damage increases by {@dice 1d10} for each spell slot level above 3.
Crimson Harvest.name: Crimson Harvest
Crimson Lash.entries.0: As part of casting this spell, you must expend one {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} or the spell automatically fails. A writhing lash of coagulated blood springs from your hand with the following properties:
Crimson Lash.entries.1.items.0: It counts as a Simple weapon with which you are proficient.
Crimson Lash.entries.1.items.1: It has the Light, Finesse, and Reach properties.
Crimson Lash.entries.1.items.2: You can use your spellcasting ability modifier instead of your Strength or Dexterity modifier for the attack and damage rolls with this weapon.
Crimson Lash.entries.1.items.3: Its damage die is the same as the {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} expended to cast the spell, and the damage dealt is Necrotic.
Crimson Lash.entries.1.items.4: Each time a creature takes damage from this weapon, its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum decreases by an amount equal to the damage taken.
Crimson Lash.entriesHigherLevel.0.entrie: If you cast this spell using a level 3-4 spell slot, you can make two weapon attacks with this weapon when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action. If you use a level 5+ spell slot, you can make three weapon attacks with this weapon when you take the {@action Attack|XPHB} action.
Crimson Lash.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Crimson Lash.name: Crimson Lash
Crooked Ward.entries.0: You touch an immobile surface and mark a symbol that causes agony in twisted creatures. An {@condition Invisible|XPHB}, 30-foot radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} springs out from the point you touch. Each Aberration, Fey, Fiend, Monstrosity, or Undead in the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on ability checks and attack rolls. When an affected creature enters the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there, it must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of its next turn.
Crooked Ward.entriesHigherLevel.0.entrie: You can maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB} on this spell for longer if you cast it using a spell slot of level 4 (up to 1 hour) or 5 (up to 8 hours). If you use a level 6+ spell slot, the spell doesn't require {@status Concentration|XPHB}, and the duration becomes 24 hours.
Crooked Ward.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Crooked Ward.name: Crooked Ward
Crown of Madness.entries.0: One creature that you can see within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for the duration. The creature succeeds automatically if it isn't Humanoid.
Crown of Madness.entries.1: A spectral crown appears on the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} target's head, and it must use its action before moving on each of its turns to make a melee attack against a creature other than itself that you mentally choose. The target can act normally on its turn if you choose no creature or if no creature is within its reach. The target repeats the save at the end of each of its turns, ending the spell on itself on a success.
Crown of Madness.entries.2: On your later turns, you must take the {@action Magic|XPHB} action to maintain control of the target, or the spell ends.
Crown of Madness.name: Crown of Madness
Crown of Radiance.entries.0: A flaming crown of holy light adorns your head until the spell ends. A Fiend, Fey, or Undead that moves within 30 feet of you or begins its turn within 30 feet of you takes {@damage 2d8} Radiant damage.
Crown of Radiance.entries.1: The crown sheds {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 30-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 30 feet.
Crown of Radiance.name: Crown of Radiance
Crown of Stars.entries.0: Seven star-like motes of light appear and orbit your head until the spell ends. You can use a bonus action to send one of the motes streaking toward one creature or object within 120 feet of you. When you do so, make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 4d12} radiant damage. Whether you hit or miss, the mote is expended. The spell ends early if you expend the last mote.
Crown of Stars.entries.1: If you have four or more motes remaining, they shed bright light in a 30-foot radius and dim light for an additional 30 feet. If you have one to three motes remaining, they shed dim light in a 30-foot radius.
Crown of Stars.entriesHigherLevel.0.entr: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 8th level or higher, the number of motes created increases by two for each slot level above 7th.
Crown of Stars.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: На более высоких уровнях
Crown of Stars.name: Crown of Stars
Crusader's Mantle.entries.0: You radiate a magical aura in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. While in the aura, you and your allies each deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Radiant damage when hitting with a weapon or an {@variantrule Unarmed Strike|XPHB}.
Crusader's Mantle.name: Crusader's Mantle
Culling Sickle.entries.0: You create a ghostly sickle that lasts for the duration and can strike out at your foes. The sickle appears hovering in your space and moves with you. When you cast this spell and as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} on each of your turns, you can direct it to fly at and strike a creature within 30 feet of you. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 2d8} Necrotic damage, and you gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to half the damage dealt. After the sickle hits or misses, it returns to hover in your space.
Culling Sickle.entriesHigherLevel.0.entr: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 3.
Culling Sickle.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Culling Sickle.name: Culling Sickle
Cure Wounds.entries.0: A creature you touch regains a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to {@dice 2d8} plus your spellcasting ability modifier.
Cure Wounds.entriesHigherLevel.0.entries: The healing increases by {@scaledice 2d8|1-9|2d8} for each spell slot level above 1.
Cure Wounds.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Cure Wounds.name: Cure Wounds
a thread from the bed or clothes of your target, a handful of teeth, and a gilded mirror worth 1,000+ GP, all of which the spell consumes
Curse of Conceited Obsession.entries.0: This spell curses a creature with an obsessive self-infatuation. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. Successful {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB}. On a successful save, the target takes {@damage 4d6} Psychic damage and is aware it was targeted by a curse. Additionally, for 1 minute, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage at the start of each of its turns.
Curse of Conceited Obsession.entries.1.e: On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 8d10} Psychic damage and is cursed.
Curse of Conceited Obsession.entries.2.e: The next time the cursed creature looks into a mirror or reflective surface, the surface cracks or become distorted in some way, and the curse advances to Stage 1.
Curse of Conceited Obsession.entries.3.e: The cursed creature is compelled to stop and admire itself whenever it sees its reflection. It constantly fusses over its appearance.
Curse of Conceited Obsession.entries.4.e: The cursed creature becomes obsessed with looking for its reflection wherever it goes, including in the eyes of its foes. Attacks against the cursed creature have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB}.
Curse of Conceited Obsession.entries.5.e: The cursed creature is driven to find or build a location where it can see its own reflection from many angles. The cursed creature is compelled to remain in this location and admire itself. Additionally, the cursed creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls from its obsession with its own appearance.
Curse of Conceited Obsession.entries.6.e: The cursed creature twists into a deformed figure and becomes a Weeping Willow.
Curse of Conceited Obsession.name: Curse of Conceited Obsession
Curse of Crushing Sensation.components.m: a drop of the target's blood, a shred of silk material, and a horn worth 1,000+ GP, all of which the spell consumes
Curse of Crushing Sensation.entries.0: This spell curses a creature with painful hypersensitivity to physical sensations. The target must make a Constitution saving throw.
Curse of Crushing Sensation.entries.1.en: On a successful save, the target takes {@damage 4d6} Thunder damage and is aware it was targeted by a curse. For 1 minute, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Thunder damage at the start of each of its turns.
Curse of Crushing Sensation.entries.2.en: On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 8d10} Thunder damage and is cursed.
Curse of Crushing Sensation.entries.3.en: The next time the target hears a loud noise, it gets a crippling headache, and the curse advances to Stage 1.
Curse of Crushing Sensation.entries.4.en: The cursed creature avoids {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}, loud noises, extreme temperatures, and doesn't like to be touched.
Curse of Crushing Sensation.entries.5.en: The cursed creature can't stand the feeling of rough or heavy clothing against its skin. It can't wear armor. It has {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Thunder damage from its hypersensitivity. Additionally, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls if it or its target is in an area of {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB}.
Curse of Crushing Sensation.entries.6.en: Even mild sensory input becomes intolerable to the cursed creature. It is driven to find a dark and quiet lair, such as a deep cave or dungeon. The cursed creature can't willingly leave this lair and has {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to all damage except for Psychic damage while outside its lair.
Curse of Crushing Sensation.entries.7.en: The cursed creature twists into a deformed figure and becomes a Sightless Agony.
Curse of Crushing Sensation.name: Curse of Crushing Sensation
Curse of Damned Aging.components.m: a childhood possession of the target, a pint of blood, and a watch worth 600+ GP, all of which the spell consumes
Curse of Damned Aging.entries.0: This spell curses a creature with horrific aging. The target must make a Strength saving throw.
On a successful save, the target takes {@damage 2d10} Necrotic damage and is aware it was targeted by a curse. For 1 minute, the target has {@variantrule Vulnerability|XPHB} to Bludgeoning, Piercing, or Slashing damage (you choose when you cast the spell).
Curse of Damned Aging.entries.1.name: Successful Saving Throw
On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 6d10} Necrotic damage and is cursed.
Curse of Damned Aging.entries.2.name: Initial Effect
The next time the target finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, it finds multiple wrinkles, grey hairs, or spots it didn't previously have, and the curse advances to Stage 1.
Curse of Damned Aging.entries.3.name: Triggering Event
The cursed creature is compelled to rest when the occasion permits it, becoming lethargic and lazy.
Curse of Damned Aging.entries.4.name: Stage 1
The cursed creature becomes feeble. After finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}, the creature can only use 25 percent of its {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}, rounded up, to regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}.
Curse of Damned Aging.entries.5.name: Stage 2
The cursed creature starts to age rapidly, becoming decrepit. The cursed creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Strength, Dexterity, and Constitution saving throws.
Curse of Damned Aging.entries.6.name: Stage 3
The cursed creature twists into a deformed figure and becomes a Body Snatcher.
Curse of Damned Aging.entries.7.name: Culmination
Curse of Damned Aging.name: Curse of Damned Aging
Curse of Fastidious Pride.components.m: a shred of the target's clothing, an uncrushed dead spider, and a sewing needle worth 600+ GP, all of which the spell consumes
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.0: This spell curses a creature with narcissistic hubris.
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.1: The target must make an Intelligence saving throw. Successful {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB}. On a successful save, the target takes {@damage 2d10} Psychic damage and is aware it was targeted by a curse.
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.2.entr: On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 4d10} Psychic damage, has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition for 1 minute, and is cursed.
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.2.name: Initial Effect
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.3.entr: The next time the target hears its name spoken, the speaker sounds derisive regardless of its actual tone, and the curse advances to Stage 1.
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.3.name: Triggering Event
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.4.entr: The cursed creature hears veiled insults everywhere, believing everyone is jealous of its brilliance and talent.
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.4.name: Stage 1
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.5.entr: The cursed creature feels restless unless it is actively working to accomplish a goal. The cursed creature gains no benefit from its {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short Rests}.
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.5.name: Stage 2
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.6.entr: The cursed creature believes everyone is involved in a conspiracy to bring about its downfall. Whenever it fails a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB}, it has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the next {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} it makes.
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.6.name: Stage 3
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.7.entr: The cursed creature twists into a deformed figure and becomes a Mind Siphon.
Curse of Fastidious Pride.entries.7.name: Culmination
Curse of Fastidious Pride.name: Curse of Fastidious Pride
Curse of Foul Blight.components.m: a shred of the target's hair or flesh, a chunk of rotten meat, and a jewelry box worth 600+ GP, all of which the spell consumes
Curse of Foul Blight.entries.0: This spell curses a creature with a putrefying and stinking pox. The target must make a Charisma saving throw. Successful {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB}. On a successful save, the target takes {@damage 3d10} Necrotic damage and is aware it was targeted by a curse. Additionally, it can't gain {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} or regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} for 1 minute.
Curse of Foul Blight.entries.1.entries.0: On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 6d10} Necrotic damage and is cursed.
Curse of Foul Blight.entries.1.name: Initial Effect
Curse of Foul Blight.entries.2.entries.0: The next time the target searches for its garments or equipment, it finds an infestation of insects, and the curse advances to Stage 1.
Curse of Foul Blight.entries.2.name: Triggering Event
Curse of Foul Blight.entries.3.entries.0: The cursed creature is afflicted with a minor cough. Whenever it speaks more than a few words at a time, it breaks into a fit of coughing. This does not affect spellcasting.
Curse of Foul Blight.entries.3.name: Stage 1
Curse of Foul Blight.entries.4.entries.0: The smell of putrescence lingers in the air around the cursed creature. Food and drink quickly spoil, jewelry tarnishes, and wood rots within 10 feet of the cursed creature. Additionally, the cursed creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Charisma ability checks and saving throws, and it can't maintain {@status Concentration|XPHB}.
Curse of Foul Blight.entries.4.name: Stage 2
Curse of Foul Blight.entries.5.entries.0: The cursed creature's skin becomes riddled with pockmarks, pustules, and lesions. Insects flock to the cursed creature, infesting its clothes. The cursed creature can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} except by spending {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} after finishing a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB}.
Curse of Foul Blight.entries.5.name: Stage 3
Curse of Foul Blight.entries.6.entries.0: The cursed creature twists into a deformed figure and becomes a Plague Carrion.
Curse of Foul Blight.entries.6.name: Culmination
Curse of Foul Blight.name: Curse of Foul Blight
Curse of Ill-Fated Fortune.components.m: a discarded piece of the target's equipment, a splinter of bone, and a collection of dice worth 800+ GP, all of which the spell consumes
Curse of Ill-Fated Fortune.entries.0: This spell curses a creature with lethal bad luck. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw.
Curse of Ill-Fated Fortune.entries.1.ent: On a successful save, the target takes {@damage 3d10} Thunder damage and is aware it was targeted by a curse. Additionally,  the creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on the first {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} it makes in the next 1 minute.
Curse of Ill-Fated Fortune.entries.2.ent: On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 7d10} Thunder damage and is cursed.
Curse of Ill-Fated Fortune.entries.3.ent: The next time the target walks through a doorway, the target stubs its toe or bashes its head, and the curse advances to Stage 1.
Curse of Ill-Fated Fortune.entries.4.ent: The cursed creature falls victim to minor inconveniences and bad luck. Shops it wishes to visit close just as it arrives, and its equipment breaks at inconvenient moments.
Curse of Ill-Fated Fortune.entries.5.ent: The cursed creature becomes a beacon of bad luck as otherwise harmless setbacks become increasingly dangerous. Objects constantly seem to be placed in the creature's way. Additionally, the cursed creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Dexterity ability checks and saving throws.
Curse of Ill-Fated Fortune.entries.6.ent: The cursed creature becomes dangerously prone to accidents, as even the simplest tasks have unforeseen life-threatening consequences. The cursed creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Initiative|XPHB} checks, its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 10 feet, and it has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition if it misses an attack roll.
Curse of Ill-Fated Fortune.entries.7.ent: The cursed creature twists into a deformed figure and becomes a Herald of Calamity.
Curse of Ill-Fated Fortune.name: Curse of Ill-Fated Fortune
Curse of Insatiable Greed.components.m: a coin that the target has previously possessed, the tail of a rat, and a golden crown of thorns worth 800+ GP, all of which the spell consumes
Curse of Insatiable Greed.entries.0: This spell curses a creature with a bitter and self-destructive greed. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw. Successful {@variantrule Saving Throw|XPHB}. On a successful save, the target takes {@damage 4d10} Cold damage and is aware it was targeted by a curse.
Curse of Insatiable Greed.entries.1.entr: On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 6d10} Cold damage, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is reduced to 0 for 1 minute. At the end of each turns, the target's {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} increases by 5 feet, until it reaches its maximum {@variantrule Speed|XPHB}.
Curse of Insatiable Greed.entries.1.name: Initial Effect
Curse of Insatiable Greed.entries.2.entr: The next time the target finishes a {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short} or {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, it notices it has lost a valued item, and the curse advances to Stage 1.
Curse of Insatiable Greed.entries.2.name: Triggering Event
Curse of Insatiable Greed.entries.3.entr: The cursed creature becomes compelled to steal trinkets.
Curse of Insatiable Greed.entries.3.name: Stage 1
Curse of Insatiable Greed.entries.4.entr: The cursed creature finds a secret place to start stashing its stolen trinkets, and it becomes anxious about leaving the location for extended periods of time. The creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Wisdom ability checks and saving throws.
Curse of Insatiable Greed.entries.4.name: Stage 2
Curse of Insatiable Greed.entries.5.entr: The cursed creature is driven to transform its hoard of trinkets into a labyrinth of possessions and treasure. The cursed creature desires to remain in this lair. The creature can't take {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions}.
Curse of Insatiable Greed.entries.5.name: Stage 3
Curse of Insatiable Greed.entries.6.entr: The cursed creature twists into a deformed figure and becomes a Verminous Abomination.
Curse of Insatiable Greed.entries.6.name: Culmination
Curse of Insatiable Greed.name: Curse of Insatiable Greed
Curse of Lost Sentiment.components.m: a lock of hair from someone the target loves, an animal's heart, and an idol worth 600+ GP, all of which the spell consumes
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.0: This spell curses a creature with the loss of memories and horrific madness. The target must make an Intelligence saving throw.
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.1.entrie: On a successful save, the target takes {@damage 3d10} Force damage, has the {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn, and is unaware it was targeted by a curse.
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.1.name: Successful Saving Throw
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.2.entrie: On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 6d10} Force damage, has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn, and is cursed.
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.2.name: Initial Effect
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.3.entrie: The next time the target finishes a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, it suffers nightmarish visions of being abandoned or left alone, and the curse advances to Stage 1.
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.3.name: Triggering Event
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.4.entrie: The cursed creature begins forgetting events that have occurred within the past few days and the names of acquaintances.
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.4.name: Stage 1
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.5.entrie: The cursed creature forgets all but its closest companions, and it has delusions of hidden threats and scheming rivals. Additionally, the cursed creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Intelligence ability checks and saving throws.
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.5.name: Stage 2
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.6.entrie: The cursed creature forgets its closest companions, its own identity, and its goals. The curse fabricates delusions of a great conspiracy that only the cursed creature can prevent. The cursed creature is compelled to take any action it believes necessary to uncover this conspiracy. The creature can't take {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB|Bonus Actions}.
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.6.name: Stage 3
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.7.entrie: The cursed creature twists into a deformed figure and becomes a Dream Whisperer.
Curse of Lost Sentiment.entries.7.name: Culmination
Curse of Lost Sentiment.name: Curse of Lost Sentiment
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.components.m: a morsel of food that belonged to the target, a sun-dried slug, and a dining plate worth 400+ GP, all of which the spell consumes
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.0: This spell curses a creature with painful and unending starvation. The target must make a Constitution saving throw.
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.1.entri: On a successful save, the target takes {@damage 2d10} Poison damage, has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn, and is aware it was targeted by a curse.
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.1.name: Successful Saving Throw
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.2.entri: On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 4d10} Poison damage, has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition for 1 minute, and is cursed.
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.2.name: Initial Effect
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.3.entri: The next time it eats a meal, the cursed creature bites its tongue and its mouth fills with blood, and the curse advances to Stage 1.
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.3.name: Triggering Event
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.4.entri: The cursed creature is gripped with an insatiable appetite.
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.4.name: Stage 1
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.5.entri: The cursed creature becomes compelled to eat inappropriate items such as coins, flowers, glass, and dirt.
