Additionally, the cursed creature has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Constitution saving throws, and its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point} maximum decreases by an amount equal to its character level (or {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice} if it has no character level).
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.5.name: Stage 2
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.6.entri: The cursed creature becomes ravenous and is compelled to consume the flesh of Humanoids. No other food satiates it. The creature can't have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests}.
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.6.name: Stage 3
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.7.entri: The cursed creature twists into a deformed figure and becomes a Bloated Gastromorph.
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.entries.7.name: Culmination
Curse of Ravenous Hunger.name: Curse of Ravenous Hunger
Curse of Uncontrollable Wrath.components: blood or other fluid of the target, a severed hand, and a serrated knife worth 1,000+ GP, all of which the spell consumes
Curse of Uncontrollable Wrath.entries.0: This spell curses a creature with an uncontrollable temper and a lust for violence. The target must make a Wisdom saving throw.
On a successful save, the target takes {@damage 4d10} Psychic damage and is aware it was targeted by a curse. Additionally, for 1 minute, the target has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attacks made using {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB|Reactions}.
On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 8d10} Psychic damage and is cursed.
The next time the cursed creature attacks with a weapon or casts a spell using a {@variantrule Spellcasting Focus|XPHB}, an old wound reopens, and the curse advances to Stage 1.
The cursed creature becomes easily agitated and aggressive.
The cursed creature becomes obsessed with violence. The thrill of solo battle intoxicates it, and its demeanor toward its allies sours as the curse grows. The cursed creature gains no benefit from its {@variantrule Short Rest|XPHB|Short Rests}.
The cursed creature is driven to insatiable bloodlust, unable to rest while there are enemies to slaughter. The cursed creature gains no benefit from its {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB|Long Rests}.
The cursed creature twists into a deformed figure and becomes an Avatar of Slaughter.
Curse of Uncontrollable Wrath.name: Curse of Uncontrollable Wrath
Cursed Cacophany.entries.0: You focus on a creature that you are familiar with that is on the same plane of existence as you. The target must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be cursed for the duration. Once a target fails its saving throw against this spell, you can't target it with a ritual casting of the spell for 24 hours.
Cursed Cacophany.entries.1: While cursed, the target hears haunting, discordant music and has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB|D20 Tests}. The first time the target fails a {@variantrule D20 Test|XPHB} during a turn, it repeats the save against this spell with neither {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} nor {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB}, ending the spell early on a success.
Cursed Cacophany.name: Cursed Cacophany
Dancing Lights.entries.0: You create up to four torch-size lights within range, making them appear as torches, lanterns, or glowing orbs that hover for the duration. Alternatively, you combine the four lights into one glowing Medium form that is vaguely humanlike. Whichever form you choose, each light sheds {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius.
Dancing Lights.entries.1: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can move the lights up to 60 feet to a space within range. A light must be within 20 feet of another light created by this spell, and a light vanishes if it exceeds the spell's range.
Dancing Lights.name: Dancing Lights
Danse Macabre.entries.0: Threads of dark power leap from your fingers to pierce up to five Small or Medium corpses you can see within range. Each corpse immediately stands up and becomes undead. You decide whether it is a {@creature zombie} or a {@creature skeleton} (the statistics for {@creature zombie||zombies} and {@creature skeleton||skeletons} are in the Monster Manual), and it gains a bonus to its attack and damage rolls equal to your spellcasting ability modifier.
Danse Macabre.entries.1: You can use a bonus action to mentally command the creatures you make with this spell, issuing the same command to all of them. To receive the command, a creature must be within 60 feet of you. You decide what action the creatures will take and where they will move during their next turn, or you can issue a general command, such as to guard a chamber or passageway against your foes. If you issue no commands, the creatures do nothing except defend themselves against hostile creatures. Once given an order, the creatures continue to follow it until their task is complete.
Danse Macabre.entries.2: The creatures are under your control until the spell ends, after which they become inanimate once more.
Danse Macabre.entriesHigherLevel.0.entri: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, you animate up to two additional corpses for each slot level above 5th.
Danse Macabre.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: На более высоких уровнях
Danse Macabre.name: Danse Macabre
Dark Sacrament.components.m: a dagger encrusted in jewels worth 100+ GP
Dark Sacrament.entries.0: As part of casting this spell, you must expend four {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} or the spell automatically fails. Make a melee spell attack against a creature within 5 feet using the Material component of this spell. On a hit, roll the {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB} expended to cast the spell plus {@dice 4d8}. You deal Necrotic damage equal to the roll's total. If this damage causes the creature to be reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it immediately dies and you gain one of the following dark blessings of your choice. When you take Radiant damage while you have a dark blessing, you take an extra {@damage 1d4} Radiant damage.
Dark Sacrament.entries.1.entries.0: You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on all saving throws.
Dark Sacrament.entries.1.name: Unassailable
Dark Sacrament.entries.2.entries.0: Your size increases by one category (from Medium to Large, for example), you gain a number of {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} equal to your Constitution modifier (minimum 1) at the start of each of your turns, and your weapon attacks deal an extra {@damage 1d4} damage.
Dark Sacrament.entries.2.name: Unbreakable
Dark Sacrament.entries.3.name: Unerring
Dark Sacrament.entries.4: Your dark blessing ends after 10 minutes or when you are reduced to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, whichever happens first.
Dark Sacrament.entriesHigherLevel.0.entr: The initial damage increases by {@dice 1d8} for each spell slot level above 4.
Dark Sacrament.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Dark Sacrament.name: Dark Sacrament
Dark Star.components.m: a shard of onyx and a drop of the caster's blood, both of which the spell consumes
Dark Star.entries.0: This spell creates a sphere centered on a point you choose within range. The sphere can have a radius of up to 40 feet. The area within this sphere is filled with magical darkness and crushing gravitational force.
Dark Star.entries.1: For the duration, the spell's area is {@quickref difficult terrain||3}. A creature with {@sense darkvision} can't see through the magical darkness, and nonmagical light can't illuminate it. No sound can be created within or pass through the area. Any creature or object entirely inside the sphere is immune to thunder damage, and creatures are {@condition deafened} while entirely inside it. Casting a spell that includes a verbal component is impossible there.
Dark Star.entries.2: Any creature that enters the spell's area for the first time on a turn or starts its turn there must make a Constitution saving throw. The creature takes {@damage 8d10} force damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature reduced to 0 hit points by this damage is disintegrated. A disintegrated creature and everything it is wearing and carrying, except magic items, are reduced to a pile of fine gray dust.
Dark Star.name: Dark Star
Darkness.components.m: мех летучей мыши и кусок угля
Darkness.entries.0: Пока заклинание действует, из точки в пределах дистанции расползается магическая {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB|Темнота}, образуя 15-футовую {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Сферу}. {@sense Darkvision|XPHB|Тёмное зрение} не может видеть сквозь эту тьму, и немагический свет не может осветить эту область.
Darkness.entries.1: Вместо нацеливания на точку вы можете сотворить это заклинание на объект, который никто не несёт и не носит; в таком случае {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB|тьма} образует 15-футовую {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Эманацию}, исходящую от этого объекта. Если полностью накрыть источник тьмы непрозрачным предметом, например, чашей или шлемом, {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB|тьма} будет заблокирована.
Darkness.entries.2: Если область воздействия этого заклинания пересекается с зоной {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB|Яркого} или {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB|Тусклого света}, созданной заклинанием 2-го уровня или ниже, то создавшее свет заклинание рассеивается.
Darkness.name: Темнота
Darkvision.entries.0: For the duration, a willing creature you touch has {@sense Darkvision|XPHB} with a range of 150 feet.
Darkvision.name: Тёмное зрение
Dawn.components.m: a sunburst pendant worth at least 100 gp
Dawn.entries.0: The light of dawn shines down on a location you specify within range. Until the spell ends, a 30-foot-radius, 40-foot-high cylinder of bright light glimmers there. This light is sunlight.
Dawn.entries.1: When the cylinder appears, each creature in it must make a Constitution saving throw, taking {@damage 4d10} radiant damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. A creature must also make this saving throw whenever it ends its turn in the cylinder.
Dawn.entries.2: If you're within 60 feet of the cylinder, you can move it up to 60 feet as a bonus action on your turn.
Dawn.name: Dawn
Daylight.entries.0: For the duration, sunlight spreads from a point within range and fills a 60-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}. The sunlight's area is {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} and sheds {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 60 feet.
Daylight.entries.1: Alternatively, you cast the spell on an object that isn't being worn or carried, causing the sunlight to fill a 60-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from that object. Covering that object with something opaque, such as a bowl or helm, blocks the sunlight.
Daylight.entries.2: If any of this spell's area overlaps with an area of {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} created by a spell of level 3 or lower, that other spell is dispelled.
Daylight.name: Daylight
Dazing Blast.entries.0: A wave of force leaves your palms, targeting a creature within range. The target makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 2d6} Force damage and has the {@condition Stunned|XPHB} condition until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, the creature takes the damage only.
Dazing Blast.entriesHigherLevel.0.entrie: One additional creature can be targeted for each spell slot above 2.
Dazing Blast.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Dazing Blast.name: Dazing Blast
Death Armor.components.m: an onyx worth 50+ GP, which the spell consumes
Death Armor.entries.0: For the duration, an inky aura surrounds one creature you touch. The target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on {@variantrule Death Saving Throw|XPHB|Death Saving Throws}, and once per turn, when a creature within 5 feet of the target hits it with a melee attack roll, the attacker takes {@damage 2d4} Necrotic damage.
Death Armor.name: Death Armor
Death Ward.entries.0: You touch a creature and grant it a measure of protection from death. The first time the target would drop to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} before the spell ends, the target instead drops to 1 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB|Hit Point}, and the spell ends.
Death Ward.entries.1: If the spell is still in effect when the target is subjected to an effect that would kill it instantly without dealing damage, that effect is negated against the target, and the spell ends.
Death Ward.name: Death Ward
Delayed Blast Fireball.components.m: a ball of bat guano and sulfur
Delayed Blast Fireball.entries.0: A beam of yellow light flashes from you, then condenses at a chosen point within range as a glowing bead for the duration. When the spell ends, the bead explodes, and each creature in a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} centered on that point makes a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes Fire damage equal to the total accumulated damage on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
Delayed Blast Fireball.entries.1: The spell's base damage is {@damage 12d6}, and the damage increases by {@damage 1d6} whenever your turn ends and the spell hasn't ended.
Delayed Blast Fireball.entries.2: If a creature touches the glowing bead before the spell ends, that creature makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the spell ends, causing the bead to explode. On a successful save, the creature can throw the bead up to 40 feet. If the thrown bead enters a creature's space or collides with a solid object, the spell ends, and the bead explodes.
Delayed Blast Fireball.entries.3: When the bead explodes, flammable objects in the explosion that aren't being worn or carried start {@hazard burning|XPHB}.
Delayed Blast Fireball.entriesHigherLeve: The base damage increases by {@scaledamage 12d6|7-9|1d6} for each spell slot level above 7.
Delayed Blast Fireball.name: Delayed Blast Fireball
Demiplane.entries.0: You create a shadowy Medium door on a flat solid surface that you can see within range. This door can be opened and closed, and it leads to a demiplane that is an empty room 30 feet in each dimension, made of wood or stone (your choice).
Demiplane.entries.1: When the spell ends, the door vanishes, and any objects inside the demiplane remain there. Any creatures inside also remain unless they opt to be shunted through the door as it vanishes, landing with the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition in the unoccupied spaces closest to the door's former space.
Demiplane.entries.2: Each time you cast this spell, you can create a new demiplane or connect the shadowy door to a demiplane you created with a previous casting of this spell. Additionally, if you know the nature and contents of a demiplane created by a casting of this spell by another creature, you can connect the shadowy door to that demiplane instead.
Demiplane.name: Demiplane
Deryan's Helpful Homunculi.components.m: powdered gemstones worth 100+ GP, which the spell consumes, and one set of Artisan's Tools with which you have proficiency
Deryan's Helpful Homunculi.entries.0: You summon a group of helpful spirits, which lasts for the duration. The spirits appear as homunculi or as another Construct of your choice but are intangible and invulnerable, and they are considered to have proficiency in the {@skill Arcana|XPHB} skill and with the set of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} used in the spell's casting.
Deryan's Helpful Homunculi.entries.1: If you are crafting an item, the spirits function as a single assistant for your crafting, halving the crafting time.
Deryan's Helpful Homunculi.name: Deryan's Helpful Homunculi
Destructive Wave.entries.0: Destructive energy ripples outward from you in a 30-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. Each creature you choose in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 5d6} Thunder damage and {@damage 5d6} Radiant or Necrotic damage (your choice) and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. On a successful save, a target takes half as much damage only.
Destructive Wave.name: Destructive Wave
Detect Evil and Good.entries.0: For the duration, you sense the location of any Aberration, Celestial, Elemental, Fey, Fiend, or Undead within 30 feet of yourself. You also sense whether the {@spell Hallow|XPHB} spell is active there and, if so, where.
Detect Evil and Good.entries.1: The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.
Detect Evil and Good.name: Detect Evil and Good
Detect Magic.entries.0: For the duration, you sense the presence of magical effects within 30 feet of yourself. If you sense such effects, you can take the {@action Magic|XPHB} action to see a faint aura around any visible creature or object in the area that bears the magic, and if an effect was created by a spell, you learn the spell's {@book school of magic|XPHB|7|Schools of Magic}.
Detect Magic.entries.1: The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.
Detect Magic.name: Detect Magic
Detect Poison and Disease.entries.0: For the duration, you sense the location of poisons, poisonous or venomous creatures, and magical contagions within 30 feet of yourself. You sense the kind of poison, creature, or contagion in each case.
Detect Poison and Disease.entries.1: The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.
Detect Poison and Disease.name: Detect Poison and Disease
Detect Thoughts.entries.0: You activate one of the effects below. Until the spell ends, you can activate either effect as a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your later turns.
Detect Thoughts.entries.1.entries.0: You sense the presence of thoughts within 30 feet of yourself that belong to creatures that know languages or are telepathic. You don't read the thoughts, but you know that a thinking creature is present.
Detect Thoughts.entries.1.entries.1: The spell is blocked by 1 foot of stone, dirt, or wood; 1 inch of metal; or a thin sheet of lead.
Detect Thoughts.entries.1.name: Sense Thoughts
Detect Thoughts.entries.2.entries.0: Target one creature you can see within 30 feet of yourself or one creature within 30 feet of yourself that you detected with the Sense Thoughts option. You learn what is most on the target's mind right now. If the target doesn't know any languages and isn't telepathic, you learn nothing.
Detect Thoughts.entries.2.entries.1: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action on your next turn, you can try to probe deeper into the target's mind. If you probe deeper, the target makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, you discern the target's reasoning, emotions, and something that looms large in its mind (such as a worry, love, or hate). On a successful save, the spell ends. Either way, the target knows that you are probing into its mind, and until you shift your attention away from the target's mind, the target can take an action on its turn to make an Intelligence ({@skill Arcana|XPHB}) check against your spell save DC, ending the spell on a success.
Detect Thoughts.entries.2.name: Read Thoughts
Detect Thoughts.name: Detect Thoughts
Devil's Due.entries.0: You absorb a measure of a creature's suffering and escaping vital essence. Expend and roll one {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Points Die}. You regain a number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} equal to the number rolled plus your spellcasting ability modifier.
Devil's Due.entries.1: If the creature took damage from a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB}, double the number of {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} you regain.
Devil's Due.entriesHigherLevel.0.entries: You can expend one additional {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Points Die} for each spell slot level above 1.
Devil's Due.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Devil's Due.name: Devil's Due
Dimension Door.entries.0: You teleport to a location within range. You arrive at exactly the spot desired. It can be a place you can see, one you can visualize, or one you can describe by stating distance and direction, such as "200 feet straight downward" or "300 feet upward to the northwest at a 45-degree angle."
Dimension Door.entries.1: You can also teleport one willing creature. The creature must be within 5 feet of you when you teleport, and it teleports to a space within 5 feet of your destination space.
Dimension Door.entries.2: If you, the other creature, or both would arrive in a space occupied by a creature or completely filled by one or more objects, you and any creature traveling with you each take {@damage 4d6} Force damage, and the teleportation fails.
Dimension Door.name: Dimension Door
Dirge.entries.0: Deathly power fills a 60-foot {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} originating from you for the duration.
Dirge.entries.1: When you cast this spell, you can designate creatures to be unaffected by it. Any other creature can't regain {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} while in the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation}. Whenever the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} enters a creature's space and whenever a creature enters the {@variantrule Emanation [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Emanation} or ends its turn there, the creature makes a Constitution saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 3d10} Necrotic damage and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is halved. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
Dirge.entries.2.entries.0: Casting this as a Circle spell requires a minimum of two secondary casters. If the spell is cast as a Circle spell, its duration becomes {@status Concentration|XPHB}, up to 10 minutes.
Dirge.entries.2.entries.1: A creature that fails its save against the spell's effect also gains 1 {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level. While the creature has {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} levels, finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB} neither restores lost {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} nor reduces the creature's {@condition Exhaustion|XPHB} level.
Dirge.entries.2.entries.2: When the spell is cast, each secondary caster must expend a level 4+ spell slot; otherwise, the spell fails.
Dirge.entries.2.name: Casting as a Circle Spell
Dirge.name: Dirge
Disguise Self.entries.0: You make yourself—including your clothing, armor, weapons, and other belongings on your person—look different until the spell ends. You can seem 1 foot shorter or taller and can appear heavier or lighter. You must adopt a form that has the same basic arrangement of limbs as you have. Otherwise, the extent of the illusion is up to you.
Disguise Self.entries.1: The changes wrought by this spell fail to hold up to physical inspection. For example, if you use this spell to add a hat to your outfit, objects pass through the hat, and anyone who touches it would feel nothing.
Disguise Self.entries.2: To discern that you are disguised, a creature must take the {@action Study|XPHB} action to inspect your appearance and succeed on an Intelligence ({@skill Investigation|XPHB}) check against your spell save DC.
Disguise Self.name: Disguise Self
Disintegrate.entries.0: You launch a green ray at a target you can see within range. The target can be a creature, a nonmagical object, or a creation of magical force, such as the wall created by {@spell Wall of Force|XPHB}.
Disintegrate.entries.1: A creature targeted by this spell makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 10d6 + 40} Force damage. If this damage reduces it to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it and everything nonmagical it is wearing and carrying are disintegrated into gray dust. The target can be revived only by a {@spell True Resurrection|XPHB} or a {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell.
Disintegrate.entries.2: This spell automatically disintegrates a Large or smaller nonmagical object or a creation of magical force. If such a target is Huge or larger, this spell disintegrates a 10-foot-{@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} portion of it.
Disintegrate.entriesHigherLevel.0.entrie: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 10d6 + 40|6-9|3d6} for each spell slot level above 6.
Disintegrate.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Disintegrate.name: Disintegrate
Dispel Evil and Good.entries.0: For the duration, Celestials, Elementals, Fey, Fiends, and Undead have {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on attack rolls against you. You can end the spell early by using either of the following special functions.
Dispel Evil and Good.entries.1.entries.0: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you touch a creature that is possessed by or has the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition from one or more creatures of the types above. The target is no longer possessed, {@condition Charmed|XPHB}, or {@condition Frightened|XPHB} by such creatures.
Dispel Evil and Good.entries.1.name: Break Enchantment
Dispel Evil and Good.entries.2.entries.0: As a {@action Magic|XPHB} action, you target one creature you can see within 5 feet of you that has one of the creature types above. The target must succeed on a Charisma saving throw or be sent back to its home plane if it isn't there already. If they aren't on their home plane, Undead are sent to the Shadowfell, and Fey are sent to the Feywild.
Dispel Evil and Good.entries.2.name: Dismissal
Dispel Evil and Good.name: Dispel Evil and Good
Dispel Magic.entries.0: Choose one creature, object, or magical effect within range. Any ongoing spell of level 3 or lower on the target ends. For each ongoing spell of level 4 or higher on the target, make an ability check using your spellcasting ability ({@dc 10} plus that spell's level). On a successful check, the spell ends.
Dispel Magic.entriesHigherLevel.0.entrie: You automatically end a spell on the target if the spell's level is equal to or less than the level of the spell slot you use.
Dispel Magic.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Dispel Magic.name: Dispel Magic
Dissolution.entries.0: You touch a creature or nonmagical object and break the target's substance down into its most fundamental components. Make a melee spell attack. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 1d8} Force damage, and its AC is reduced by 1 against the next attack roll made against it before the end of your next turn.
Dissolution.entriesHigherLevel.0.entries: The damage increases by {@dice 1d8} when you reach levels 5 ({@dice 2d8}), 11 ({@dice 3d8}), and 17 ({@dice 4d8}).
Dissolution.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Улучшение заговора
Dissolution.name: Dissolution
Dissonant Whispers.entries.0: One creature of your choice that you can see within range hears a discordant melody in its mind. The target makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, it takes {@damage 3d6} Psychic damage and must immediately use its {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB}, if available, to move as far away from you as it can, using the safest route. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.
The damage increases by {@scaledamage 3d6|1-9|1d6} for each spell slot level above 1.
Dissonant Whispers.name: Dissonant Whispers
Distort Value.entries.0: Do you need to squeeze a few more gold pieces out of a merchant as you try to sell that weird octopus statue you liberated from the chaos temple? Do you need to downplay the worth of some magical assets when the tax collector stops by? Distort value has you covered.
Distort Value.entries.1: You cast this spell on an object no more than 1 foot on a side, doubling the object's perceived value by adding illusory flourishes or polish to it, or reducing its perceived value by half with the help of illusory scratches, dents, and other unsightly features. Anyone examining the object can ascertain its true value with a successful Intelligence ({@skill Investigation}) check against your spell save DC.
Distort Value.entriesHigherLevel.0.entri: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the maximum size of the object increases by 1 foot for each slot level above 1st.
Distort Value.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: На более высоких уровнях
Distort Value.name: Distort Value
Divination.components.m: incense worth 25+ GP, which the spell consumes
Divination.entries.0: This spell puts you in contact with a god or a god's servants. You ask one question about a specific goal, event, or activity to occur within 7 days. The DM offers a truthful reply, which might be a short phrase or cryptic rhyme. The spell doesn't account for circumstances that might change the answer, such as the casting of other spells.
Divination.entries.1: If you cast the spell more than once before finishing a {@variantrule Long Rest|XPHB}, there is a cumulative {@chance 25|||Random reading!|Regular reading} chance for each casting after the first that you get no answer.
Divination.name: Прорицание
Divine Favor.entries.0: Until the spell ends, your attacks with weapons deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Radiant damage on a hit.
Divine Favor.name: Divine Favor
Divine Smite.entries.0: The target takes an extra {@damage 2d8} Radiant damage from the attack. The damage increases by {@damage 1d8} if the target is a Fiend or an Undead.
Divine Smite.entriesHigherLevel.0.entrie: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 2d8|1-9|1d8} for each spell slot level above 1.
Divine Smite.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Divine Smite.name: Divine Smite
Divine Word.entries.0: You utter a word imbued with power from the Upper Planes. Each creature of your choice in range makes a Charisma saving throw. On a failed save, a target that has 50 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB} or fewer suffers an effect based on its current {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, as shown in the Divine Word Effects table. Regardless of its {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, a Celestial, an Elemental, a Fey, or a Fiend target that fails its save is forced back to its plane of origin (if it isn't there already) and can't return to the current plane for 24 hours by any means short of a {@spell Wish|XPHB} spell.
Divine Word.entries.1.caption: Divine Word Effects
Divine Word.entries.1.rows.1.1: The target has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB}, {@condition Deafened|XPHB}, and {@condition Stunned|XPHB} conditions for 1 hour.
Divine Word.entries.1.rows.2.1: The target has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Deafened|XPHB} conditions for 10 minutes.
Divine Word.entries.1.rows.3.1: The target has the {@condition Deafened|XPHB} condition for 1 minute.
Divine Word.name: Divine Word
Dominate Beast.entries.0: One Beast you can see within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for the duration. The target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the save if you or your allies are fighting it. Whenever the target takes damage, it repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.
Dominate Beast.entries.1: You have a telepathic link with the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} target while the two of you are on the same plane of existence. On your turn, you can use this link to issue commands to the target (no action required), such as "{@action Attack|XPHB} that creature," "Move over there," or "Fetch that object." The target does its best to obey on its turn. If it completes an order and doesn't receive further direction from you, it acts and moves as it likes, focusing on protecting itself.
Dominate Beast.entries.2: You can command the target to take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} but must take your own {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to do so.
Dominate Beast.entriesHigherLevel.0.entr: Your {@status Concentration|XPHB} can last longer with a spell slot of level 5 (up to 10 minutes), 6 (up to 1 hour), or 7+ (up to 8 hours).
Dominate Beast.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Dominate Beast.name: Dominate Beast
Dominate Monster.entries.0: One creature you can see within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for the duration. The target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the save if you or your allies are fighting it. Whenever the target takes damage, it repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.
Dominate Monster.entries.1: You have a telepathic link with the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} target while the two of you are on the same plane of existence. On your turn, you can use this link to issue commands to the target (no action required), such as "{@action Attack|XPHB} that creature," "Move over there," or "Fetch that object." The target does its best to obey on its turn. If it completes an order and doesn't receive further direction from you, it acts and moves as it likes, focusing on protecting itself.
Dominate Monster.entries.2: You can command the target to take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} but must take your own {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to do so.
Dominate Monster.entriesHigherLevel.0.en: Your {@status Concentration|XPHB} can last longer with a level 9 spell slot (up to 8 hours).
Dominate Monster.name: Dominate Monster
Dominate Person.entries.0: One Humanoid you can see within range must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or have the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} condition for the duration. The target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on the save if you or your allies are fighting it. Whenever the target takes damage, it repeats the save, ending the spell on itself on a success.
Dominate Person.entries.1: You have a telepathic link with the {@condition Charmed|XPHB} target while the two of you are on the same plane of existence. On your turn, you can use this link to issue commands to the target (no action required), such as "{@action Attack|XPHB} that creature," "Move over there," or "Fetch that object." The target does its best to obey on its turn. If it completes an order and doesn't receive further direction from you, it acts and moves as it likes, focusing on protecting itself.
Dominate Person.entries.2: You can command the target to take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} but must take your own {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to do so.
Dominate Person.entriesHigherLevel.0.ent: Your {@status Concentration|XPHB} can last longer with a spell slot of level 6 (up to 10 minutes), 7 (up to 1 hour), or 8+ (up to 8 hours).
Dominate Person.name: Dominate Person
Doomtide.entries.0: You create a 20-foot-radius {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} of inky fog within range. The fog is magical {@variantrule Darkness|XPHB} and lasts for the duration or until a strong wind (such as the one created by the {@spell Gust of Wind|XPHB} spell) disperses it, ending the spell.
Doomtide.entries.1: Each creature in the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} when it appears makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 5d6} Psychic damage and subtracts {@dice 1d6} from its saving throws until the end of its next turn. On a successful save, a creature takes half as much damage only. A creature also makes this save when the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} moves into its space, when it enters the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}, or when it ends its turn inside the {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere}. A creature makes this save only once per turn.
Doomtide.entries.2: The {@variantrule Sphere [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Sphere} moves 10 feet away from you at the start of each of your turns.
Doomtide.entries.3.entries.0: Casting this as a Circle spell requires a minimum of five secondary casters. In addition to the spell's usual components, you must provide a special component (a string of three black pearls from Pandemonium), which the spell consumes. The spell's range increases to 1 mile, and its duration increases to until dispelled (no {@status Concentration|XPHB} required). The spell ends early if any caster who participated in this casting contributes to another casting of {@spell Doomtide|FRHoF} as a Circle spell.
Doomtide.entries.3.entries.1: When the spell is cast, each secondary caster must expend a level 3+ spell slot; otherwise, the spell fails.
Doomtide.entries.3.name: Casting as a Circle Spell
Doomtide.name: Doomtide
Draconic Transformation.components.m: a statuette of a dragon, worth at least 500 gp
Draconic Transformation.entries.0: With a roar, you draw on the magic of dragons to transform yourself, taking on draconic features. You gain the following benefits until the spell ends:
You have {@sense blindsight} with a range of 30 feet. Within that range, you can effectively see anything that isn't behind {@quickref Cover||3||total cover}, even if you're {@condition blinded} or in darkness. Moreover, you can see an {@condition invisible} creature, unless the creature successfully hides from you.

When you cast this spell, and as a bonus action on subsequent turns for the duration, you can exhale shimmering energy in a 60-foot cone. Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 6d8} force damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one.
Incorporeal wings sprout from your back, giving you a flying speed of 60 feet.
Draconic Transformation.name: Draconic Transformation
Dragon's Breath.entries.0: You touch one willing creature, and choose Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Poison. Until the spell ends, the target can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to exhale a 15-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone}. Each creature in that area makes a Dexterity saving throw, taking {@damage 3d6} damage of the chosen type on a failed save or half as much damage on a successful one.
Dragon's Breath.entriesHigherLevel.0.ent: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 3d6|2-9|1d6} for each spell slot level above 2.
Dragon's Breath.name: Dragon's Breath
Drawmij's Instant Summons.entries.0: You touch the sapphire used in the casting and an object weighing 10 pounds or less whose longest dimension is 6 feet or less. The spell leaves an {@condition Invisible|XPHB} mark on that object and invisibly inscribes the object's name on the sapphire. Each time you cast this spell, you must use a different sapphire.
Drawmij's Instant Summons.entries.1: Thereafter, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to speak the object's name and crush the sapphire. The object instantly appears in your hand regardless of physical or planar distances, and the spell ends.
Drawmij's Instant Summons.entries.2: If another creature is holding or carrying the object, crushing the sapphire doesn't transport it, but instead you learn who that creature is and where that creature is currently located.
Drawmij's Instant Summons.name: Drawmij's Instant Summons
Dream of the Blue Veil.components.m: a magic item or a willing creature from the destination world
Dream of the Blue Veil.entries.0: You and up to eight willing creatures within range fall {@condition unconscious} for the spell's duration and experience visions of another world on the Material Plane, such as Oerth, Toril, Krynn, or Eberron. If the spell reaches its full duration, the visions conclude with each of you encountering and pulling back a mysterious blue curtain. The spell then ends with you mentally and physically transported to the world that was in the visions.
Dream of the Blue Veil.entries.1: To cast this spell, you must have a magic item that originated on the world you wish to reach, and you must be aware of the world's existence, even if you don't know the world's name. Your destination in the other world is a safe location within 1 mile of where the magic item was created. Alternatively, you can cast the spell if one of the affected creatures was born on the other world, which causes your destination to be a safe location within 1 mile of where that creature was born.
Dream of the Blue Veil.entries.2: The spell ends early on a creature if that creature takes any damage, and the creature isn't transported. If you take any damage, the spell ends for you and all the other creatures, with none of you being transported.
Dream of the Blue Veil.name: Dream of the Blue Veil
Dream.entries.0: You target a creature you know on the same plane of existence. You or a willing creature you touch enters a trance state to act as a dream messenger. While in the trance, the messenger is {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} and has a {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} of 0.
Dream.entries.1: If the target is asleep, the messenger appears in the target's dreams and can converse with the target as long as it remains asleep, through the spell's duration. The messenger can also shape the dream's environment, creating landscapes, objects, and other images. The messenger can emerge from the trance at any time, ending the spell. The target recalls the dream perfectly upon waking.
Dream.entries.2: If the target is awake when you cast the spell, the messenger knows it and can either end the trance (and the spell) or wait for the target to sleep, at which point the messenger enters its dreams.
Dream.entries.3: You can make the messenger terrifying to the target. If you do so, the messenger can deliver a message of no more than ten words, and then the target makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a failed save, the target gains no benefit from its rest, and it takes {@damage 3d6} Psychic damage when it wakes up.
Dream.name: Dream
Druid Grove.components.m: mistletoe, which the spell consumes, that was harvested with a golden sickle under the light of a full moon
Druid Grove.entries.0: You invoke the spirits of nature to protect an area outdoors or underground. The area can be as small as a 30-foot cube or as large as a 90-foot cube. Buildings and other structures are excluded from the affected area. If you cast this spell in the same area every day for a year, the spell lasts until dispelled.
Druid Grove.entries.1: The spell creates the following effects within the area. When you cast this spell, you can specify creatures as friends who are immune to the effects. You can also specify a password that, when spoken aloud, makes the speaker immune to these effects.
Druid Grove.entries.2: The entire warded area radiates magic. A {@spell dispel magic} cast on the area, if successful, removes only one of the following effects, not the entire area. That spell's caster chooses which effect to end. Only when all its effects are gone is this spell dispelled.
Druid Grove.entries.3.entries.0: You can fill any number of 5-foot squares on the ground with thick fog, making them heavily obscured. The fog reaches 10 feet high. In addition, every foot of movement through the fog costs 2 extra feet. To a creature immune to this effect, the fog obscures nothing and looks like soft mist, with motes of green light floating in the air.
Druid Grove.entries.3.name: Solid Fog
Druid Grove.entries.4.entries.0: You can fill any number of 5-foot squares on the ground that aren't filled with fog with grasping weeds and vines, as if they were affected by an {@spell entangle} spell. To a creature immune to this effect, the weeds and vines feel soft and reshape themselves to serve as temporary seats or beds.
Druid Grove.entries.4.name: Grasping Undergrowth
Druid Grove.entries.5.entries.0: You can animate up to four trees in the area, causing them to uproot themselves from the ground. These trees have the same statistics as an {@creature awakened tree}, which appears in the Monster Manual, except they can't speak, and their bark is covered with druidic symbols. If any creature not immune to this effect enters the warded area, the grove guardians fight until they have driven off or slain the intruders. The grove guardians also obey your spoken commands (no action required by you) that you issue while in the area. If you don't give them commands and no intruders are present, the grove guardians do nothing. The grove guardians can't leave the warded area. When the spell ends, the magic animating them disappears, and the trees take root again if possible.
Druid Grove.entries.5.name: Grove Guardians
Druid Grove.entries.6.entries.0: You can place your choice of one of the following magical effects within the warded area:
Druid Grove.entries.6.name: Additional Spell Effect
Druid Grove.entries.7.items.0: A constant {@spell gust of wind} in two locations of your choice
Druid Grove.entries.7.items.1: {@spell Spike growth} in one location of your choice
Druid Grove.entries.7.items.2: {@spell Wind wall} in two locations of your choice
Druid Grove.entries.8: To a creature immune to this effect, the winds are a fragrant, gentle breeze, and the area of {@spell spike growth} is harmless.
Druid Grove.name: Druid Grove
Druidcraft.entries.0: Шепча духам природы, вы создаёте один из следующих эффектов в пределах дистанции.
Druidcraft.entries.1.entries.0: Вы создаёте Крошечный безвредный сенсорный эффект, предсказывающий погоду в вашем местоположении в следующие 24 часа. Если погода будет ясной, эффект может выглядеть как золотистый шарик, если дождь — как тучка, падающая снежинка может обозначать снежную погоду, и так далее. Этот эффект длится 1 раунд.
Druidcraft.entries.1.name: Предсказатель погоды
Druidcraft.entries.2.entries.0: Вы немедленно заставляете цветок распуститься, почку раскрыться или семечко — прорасти.
Druidcraft.entries.2.name: Цветение
Druidcraft.entries.3.entries.0: Вы создаёте безвредный ощутимый эффект, такой как падающие листья, призрачное видение танцующих фей, дуновение ветра, звук животного, или слабый запах скунса. Эффект должен находиться в 5-футовом {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Кубе}.
Druidcraft.entries.3.name: Ощутимый эффект
Druidcraft.entries.4.entries.0: Вы зажигаете или тушите свечу, факел или костёр.
Druidcraft.entries.4.name: Игра с огнем
Druidcraft.name: Искусство друидов
Dust Devil.entries.0: Choose an unoccupied 5-foot cube of air that you can see within range. An elemental force that resembles a dust devil appears in the cube and lasts for the spell's duration.
Dust Devil.entries.1: Any creature that ends its turn within 5 feet of the dust devil must make a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 1d8} bludgeoning damage and is pushed 10 feet away from the dust devil. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage and isn't pushed.
Dust Devil.entries.2: As a bonus action, you can move the dust devil up to 30 feet in any direction. If the dust devil moves over sand, dust, loose dirt, or light gravel, it sucks up the material and forms a 10-foot-radius cloud of debris around itself that lasts until the start of your next turn. The cloud heavily obscures its area.
When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 3rd level or higher, the damage increases by {@scaledamage 1d8|2-9|1d8} for each slot level above 2nd.
Dust Devil.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: На более высоких уровнях
Dust Devil.name: Dust Devil
Earth Tremor.entries.0: You cause a tremor in the ground within range. Each creature other than you in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature takes {@damage 1d6} bludgeoning damage and is knocked {@condition prone}. If the ground in that area is loose earth or stone, it becomes {@quickref difficult terrain||3} until cleared, with each 5-foot-diameter portion requiring at least 1 minute to clear by hand.
Earth Tremor.entriesHigherLevel.0.entrie: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 2nd level or higher, the damage increases by {@scaledamage 1d6|1-9|1d6} for each slot level above 1st.
Earth Tremor.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: На более высоких уровнях
Earth Tremor.name: Earth Tremor
Earth Worm.entries.0: You transform the earth into a wormlike maw at an unoccupied point on the ground within range. As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, make a melee spell attack against a creature within 5 feet of the worm. On a hit, the target takes Piercing damage equal to {@dice 3d8} plus your spellcasting ability modifier. If the target is a Large or smaller creature, it must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or be swallowed by the worm. A swallowed creature has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Restrained|XPHB} conditions, it has {@variantrule Cover|XPHB|Total Cover} against attacks and other effects outside the worm, and it takes {@damage 6d6} Bludgeoning damage at the start of each of your turns.
Earth Worm.entries.1: A creature trapped within the worm can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against your spell save DC. If it succeeds, it is regurgitated and has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition in an unoccupied space within 10 feet of the worm.
Earth Worm.entries.2: As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you can move the worm up to 30 feet.
Earth Worm.name: Earth Worm
Earthbind.entries.0: Choose one creature you can see within range. Yellow strips of magical energy loop around the creature. The target must succeed on a Strength saving throw, or its flying speed (if any) is reduced to 0 feet for the spell's duration. An airborne creature affected by this spell safely descends at 60 feet per round until it reaches the ground or the spell ends.
Earthbind.name: Earthbind
Earthquake.entries.0: Choose a point on the ground that you can see within range. For the duration, an intense tremor rips through the ground in a 100-foot-radius circle centered on that point. The ground there is {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.
Earthquake.entries.1: When you cast this spell and at the end of each of your turns for the duration, each creature on the ground in the area makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a creature has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, and its {@status Concentration|XPHB} is broken.
Earthquake.entries.2: You can also cause the effects below.
Earthquake.entries.3.entries.0: A total of {@dice 1d6} fissures open in the spell's area at the end of the turn you cast it. You choose the fissures' locations, which can't be under structures. Each fissure is {@dice 1d10 × 10} feet deep and 10 feet wide, and it extends from one edge of the spell's area to another edge. A creature in the same space as a fissure must succeed on a Dexterity saving throw or fall in. A creature that successfully saves moves with the fissure's edge as it opens.
Earthquake.entries.3.name: Fissures
Earthquake.entries.4.entries.0: The tremor deals 50 Bludgeoning damage to any structure in contact with the ground in the area when you cast the spell and at the end of each of your turns until the spell ends. If a structure drops to 0 {@variantrule Hit Points|XPHB}, it collapses.
Earthquake.entries.4.entries.1: A creature within a distance from a collapsing structure equal to half the structure's height makes a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, the creature takes {@damage 12d6} Bludgeoning damage, has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition, and is buried in the rubble, requiring a {@dc 20} Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check as an action to escape. On a successful save, the creature takes half as much damage only.
Earthquake.entries.4.name: Structures
Earthquake.name: Earthquake
Eldritch Blast.entries.0: Вы запускаете луч потрескивающей энергии. Совершите дальнобойную атаку заклинанием по одному существу или объекту в пределах дистанции. При попадании цель получает {@damage 1d10} Силового урона.
Eldritch Blast.entriesHigherLevel.0.entr: Заклинание создаёт два луча, когда вы достигаете 5-го уровня, три луча с 11-го уровня и четыре луча с 17-го уровня. Вы можете направлять лучи как в одну цель, так и в разные. Для каждого луча совершайте отдельный бросок атаки.
Eldritch Blast.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Улучшение заговора
Eldritch Blast.name: Мистический заряд
Elemental Bane.entries.0: Choose one creature you can see within range, and choose one of the following damage types: acid, cold, fire, lightning, or thunder. The target must succeed on a Constitution saving throw or be affected by the spell for its duration. The first time each turn the affected target takes damage of the chosen type, the target takes an extra {@damage 2d6} damage of that type. Moreover, the target loses any resistance to that damage type until the spell ends.
Elemental Bane.entriesHigherLevel.0.entr: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 5th level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 4th. The creatures must be within 30 feet of each other when you target them.
Elemental Bane.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: На более высоких уровнях
Elemental Bane.name: Elemental Bane
Elemental Exhalation.entries.0: When you cast this spell, choose one of the following effects that determine the spell's damage type:
Elemental Exhalation.entries.1.items.0.e: The damage type is Thunder. Each creature that fails the saving throw is pushed 10 feet away from you.
Elemental Exhalation.entries.1.items.1.e: The damage type is Cold. Each creature that fails the saving throw has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition until the start of your next turn.
Elemental Exhalation.entries.1.items.2.e: The damage type is Bludgeoning. Each creature that fails the saving throw has its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} reduced to 0 until the end of its next turn.
Elemental Exhalation.entries.1.items.3.e: The damage type is Fire. Each creature that fails the saving throw is engulfed in flames and takes {@damage 2d6} Fire damage at the end of its next turn. As an action, it can extinguish fire on itself by giving itself the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition and rolling on the ground. The fire also goes out if it is doused, submerged, or suffocated.
Elemental Exhalation.entries.1.items.4.e: The damage type is Acid. Each creature that fails the saving throw has the {@condition Prone|XPHB} condition.
Elemental Exhalation.entries.2: Each creature within a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} of destructive elemental energy must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a failed save, a target takes {@damage 5d6} damage of the chosen type. On a successful save, the target takes half as much damage only.
The damage increases by {@dice 1d6} for each spell slot level above 3.
Elemental Exhalation.name: Elemental Exhalation
Elemental Weapon.entries.0: A nonmagical weapon you touch becomes a magic weapon. Choose one of the following damage types: Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder. For the duration, the weapon has a +1 bonus to attack rolls and deals an extra {@damage 1d4} damage of the chosen type when it hits.
Elemental Weapon.entriesHigherLevel.0.en: If you use a level 5-6 spell slot, the bonus to attack rolls increases to +2, and the extra damage increases to {@damage 2d4}. If you use a level 7+ spell slot, the bonus increases to +3, and the extra damage increases to {@damage 3d4}.
Elemental Weapon.name: Elemental Weapon
Elementalism.entries.0: You exert control over the elements, creating one of the following effects within range.
Elementalism.entries.1.entries.0: You create a breeze strong enough to ripple cloth, stir dust, rustle leaves, and close open doors and shutters, all in a 5-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}. Doors and shutters being held open by someone or something aren't affected.
Elementalism.entries.1.name: Beckon Air
Elementalism.entries.2.entries.0: You create a thin shroud of dust or sand that covers surfaces in a 5-foot-square area, or you cause a single word to appear in your handwriting in a patch of dirt or sand.
Elementalism.entries.2.name: Beckon Earth
Elementalism.entries.3.entries.0: You create a thin cloud of harmless embers and colored, scented smoke in a 5-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}. You choose the color and scent, and the embers can light candles, torches, or lamps in that area. The smoke's scent lingers for 1 minute.
Elementalism.entries.3.name: Beckon Fire
Elementalism.entries.4.entries.0: You create a spray of cool mist that lightly dampens creatures and objects in a 5-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}. Alternatively, you create 1 cup of clean water either in an open container or on a surface, and the water evaporates in 1 minute.
Elementalism.entries.4.name: Beckon Water
Elementalism.entries.5.entries.0: You cause dirt, sand, fire, smoke, mist, or water that can fit in a 1-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} to assume a crude shape (such as that of a creature) for 1 hour.
Elementalism.entries.5.name: Sculpt Element
Elementalism.name: Elementalism
Elminster's Effulgent Spheres.entries.0: Six chromatic spheres orbit you for the duration.
Elminster's Effulgent Spheres.entries.1: While the spheres are present, you can expend spheres to create the following effects:
When you take Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage, you can take a {@variantrule Reaction|XPHB} to expend one sphere and give yourself {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to the triggering damage type until the start of your next turn.

As a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}, you send one sphere hurtling toward a target within 120 feet of yourself. Make a ranged spell attack. On a hit, the target takes {@damage 3d6} Acid, Cold, Fire, Lightning, or Thunder damage (your choice). Regardless of whether you hit, the sphere is expended.
Elminster's Effulgent Spheres.entries.3: The spell ends early if you have no more spheres remaining.
Elminster's Effulgent Spheres.entriesHig: The number of spheres increases by 1 for every spell slot level above 6.
Elminster's Effulgent Spheres.name: Elminster's Effulgent Spheres
Elminster's Elusion.entries.0: Arcane wards protect you against magic for the duration. You have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws against spells and magical effects. Additionally, if you succeed on a saving throw against a spell or magical effect and would normally take half as much damage, you instead take no damage.
Elminster's Elusion.name: Elminster's Elusion
Emmeline's Essence Infusion.components.m: a diamond vial containing at least one ounce of blood worth at least 100 GP, which this spell consumes
Emmeline's Essence Infusion.entries.0: When you cast this spell, you target one nonmagical weapon within range. That weapon becomes magical and gains a special benefit based on the creature type of the blood used as the Material component for this spell. The item requires {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB}, and only the creature attuned to this weapon gains these benefits.
Emmeline's Essence Infusion.entries.1.en: Your attacks with weapons deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Psychic damage on a hit. In addition, you can telepathically speak with any creature that has taken at least 1 point of damage from this weapon in the last 24 hours, provided you are both on the same plane.
Emmeline's Essence Infusion.entries.2.en: Your attacks with weapons deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Radiant damage on a hit. In addition, you can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to cause the weapon to shed {@variantrule Bright Light|XPHB} in a 30-foot radius and {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} for an additional 30 feet. You can end this effect by using another {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Emmeline's Essence Infusion.entries.3.en: Your attack rolls with this weapon can score a {@variantrule Critical Hit|XPHB} on a roll of 19 or 20 on the {@dice d20}. In addition, you have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on saving throws you make to avoid or end the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition.
Emmeline's Essence Infusion.entries.4.en: Your attacks with weapons deal an extra {@damage 1d4} Force damage on a hit. In addition, once per turn when you hit a creature within 30 feet with this weapon, you can teleport to an unoccupied space you can see within 5 feet of it.
Emmeline's Essence Infusion.entries.5.en: Your attacks with weapons deal an extra {@damage 1d6} Necrotic damage on a hit. You can use a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB} to change this extra damage to Cold or Fire.

When you cast this spell, you attune to the magic weapon. The spell's duration lasts as long as you have {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to the weapon. During the spell's duration, the magic item is susceptible to the {@spell Dispel Magic|XPHB} spell. If you maintain {@variantrule Attunement|XPHB} to the magic weapon for 1 year, the enchantment becomes permanent and this spell ends.
Emmeline's Essence Infusion.name: Emmeline's Essence Infusion
Encode Thoughts.entries.0: Putting a finger to your head, you pull a memory, an idea, or a message from your mind and transform it into a tangible string of glowing energy called a thought strand, which persists for the duration or until you cast this spell again. The thought strand appears in an unoccupied space within 5 feet of you as a Tiny, weightless, semisolid object that can be held and carried like a ribbon. It is otherwise stationary.
Encode Thoughts.entries.1: If you cast this spell while {@status concentration||concentrating} on a spell or an ability that allows you to read or manipulate the thoughts of others (such as {@spell detect thoughts} or {@spell modify memory}), you can transform the thoughts or memories you read, rather than your own, into a thought strand.
Encode Thoughts.entries.2: Casting this spell while holding a thought strand allows you to instantly receive whatever memory, idea, or message the thought strand contains. (Casting {@spell detect thoughts} on the strand has the same effect.)
Encode Thoughts.name: Encode Thoughts
Enemies Abound.entries.0: You reach into the mind of one creature you can see and force it to make an Intelligence saving throw. A creature automatically succeeds if it is immune to being {@condition frightened}. On a failed save, the target loses the ability to distinguish friend from foe, regarding all creatures it can see as enemies until the spell ends. Each time the target takes damage, it can repeat the saving throw, ending the effect on itself on a success.
Enemies Abound.entries.1: Whenever the affected creature chooses another creature as a target, it must choose the target at random from among the creatures it can see within range of the attack, spell, or other ability it's using. If an enemy provokes an opportunity attack from the affected creature, the creature must make that attack if it is able to.
Enemies Abound.name: Enemies Abound
Enervation.entries.0: A tendril of inky darkness reaches out from you, touching a creature you can see within range to drain life from it. The target must make a Dexterity saving throw. On a successful save, the target takes {@damage 2d8} necrotic damage, and the spell ends. On a failed save, the target takes {@damage 4d8} necrotic damage, and until the spell ends, you can use your action on each of your turns to automatically deal {@damage 4d8} necrotic damage to the target. The spell ends if you use your action to do anything else, if the target is ever outside the spell's range, or if the target has {@quickref Cover||3||total cover} from you.
Enervation.entries.1: Whenever the spell deals damage to a target, you regain hit points equal to half the amount of necrotic damage the target takes.
When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 6th level or higher, the damage increases by {@scaledamage 4d8;2d8|5-9|1d8} for each slot level above 5th.
Enervation.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: На более высоких уровнях
Enervation.name: Enervation
Enhance Ability.entries.0: You touch a creature and choose Strength, Dexterity, Intelligence, Wisdom, or Charisma. For the duration, the target has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on ability checks using the chosen ability.
Enhance Ability.entriesHigherLevel.0.ent: You can target one additional creature for each spell slot level above 2. You can choose a different ability for each target.
Enhance Ability.name: Enhance Ability
Enlarge-Reduce.entries.0: For the duration, the spell enlarges or reduces a creature or an object you can see within range (see the chosen effect below). A targeted object must be neither worn nor carried. If the target is an unwilling creature, it can make a Constitution saving throw. On a successful save, the spell has no effect.
Enlarge-Reduce.entries.1: Everything that a targeted creature is wearing and carrying changes size with it. Any item it drops returns to normal size at once. A thrown weapon or piece of ammunition returns to normal size immediately after it hits or misses a target.
Enlarge-Reduce.entries.2.entries.0: The target's size increases by one category—from Medium to Large, for example. The target also has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws. The target's attacks with its enlarged weapons or Unarmed Strikes deal an extra {@damage 1d4} damage on a hit.
Enlarge-Reduce.entries.2.name: Enlarge
Enlarge-Reduce.entries.3.entries.0: The target's size decreases by one category—from Medium to Small, for example. The target also has {@variantrule Disadvantage|XPHB} on Strength checks and Strength saving throws. The target's attacks with its reduced weapons or Unarmed Strikes deal {@damage 1d4} less damage on a hit (this can't reduce the damage below 1).
Enlarge-Reduce.entries.3.name: Reduce
Enlarge-Reduce.name: Enlarge/Reduce
Ensnaring Strike.entries.0: As you hit the target, grasping vines appear on it, and it makes a Strength saving throw. A Large or larger creature has {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} on this save. On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the spell ends. On a successful save, the vines shrivel away, and the spell ends.
Ensnaring Strike.entries.1: While {@condition Restrained|XPHB}, the target takes {@damage 1d6} Piercing damage at the start of each of its turns. The target or a creature within reach of it can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against your spell save DC. On a success, the spell ends.
Ensnaring Strike.entriesHigherLevel.0.en: The damage increases by {@scaledamage 1d6|1-9|1d6} for each spell slot level above 1.
Ensnaring Strike.name: Ensnaring Strike
Enspelled Armament.components.m: a vial of oil and water
Enspelled Armament.entries.0: When you cast this spell, choose one Simple or Martial weapon. A replica of the chosen weapon, made of iridescent energy, appears in your hands. For the duration, you are proficient with that weapon and whenever you attack with the weapon, you can use your spellcasting ability modifier for the attack and damage rolls instead of using Strength or Dexterity. Attacks made with this weapon don't require ammunition, and if you throw the weapon as part of a ranged attack, it returns to your hand immediately after resolving the attack. The damage dealt by this weapon is Force instead of the weapon's normal damage type.
The damage of the weapon increases by {@dice 1d4} for each spell slot level above 2. If you cast the spell using a level 5+ spell slot, the duration becomes 8 hours.
Enspelled Armament.name: Enspelled Armament
Entangle.entries.0: Grasping plants sprout from the ground in a 20-foot square within range. For the duration, these plants turn the ground in the area into {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}. They disappear when the spell ends.
Entangle.entries.1: Each creature (other than you) in the area when you cast the spell must succeed on a Strength saving throw or have the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the spell ends. A {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against your spell save DC. On a success, it frees itself from the grasping plants and is no longer {@condition Restrained|XPHB} by them.
Entangle.name: Entangle
Enthrall.entries.0: You weave a distracting string of words, causing creatures of your choice that you can see within range to make a Wisdom saving throw. Any creature you or your companions are fighting automatically succeeds on this save. On a failed save, a target has a -10 penalty to Wisdom ({@skill Perception|XPHB}) checks and Passive {@skill Perception|XPHB} until the spell ends.
Enthrall.name: Enthrall
Erupting Earth.entries.0: Choose a point you can see on the ground within range. A fountain of churned earth and stone erupts in a 20-foot cube centered on that point. Each creature in that area must make a Dexterity saving throw. A creature takes {@damage 3d12} bludgeoning damage on a failed save, or half as much damage on a successful one. Additionally, the ground in that area becomes {@quickref difficult terrain||3} until cleared. Each 5-foot-square portion of the area requires at least 1 minute to clear by hand.
Erupting Earth.entriesHigherLevel.0.entr: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, the damage increases by {@scaledamage 3d12|3-9|1d12} for each slot level above 3rd.
Erupting Earth.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: На более высоких уровнях
Erupting Earth.name: Erupting Earth
Etherealness.entries.0: You step into the border regions of the Ethereal Plane, where it overlaps with your current plane. You remain in the Border Ethereal for the duration. During this time, you can move in any direction. If you move up or down, every foot of movement costs an extra foot. You can perceive the plane you left, which looks gray, and you can't see anything there more than 60 feet away.
Etherealness.entries.1: While on the Ethereal Plane, you can affect and be affected only by creatures, objects, and effects on that plane. Creatures that aren't on the Ethereal Plane can't perceive or interact with you unless a feature gives them the ability to do so.
Etherealness.entries.2: When the spell ends, you return to the plane you left in the spot that corresponds to your space in the Border Ethereal. If you appear in an occupied space, you are shunted to the nearest unoccupied space and take Force damage equal to twice the number of feet you are moved.
Etherealness.entries.3: This spell ends instantly if you cast it while you are on the Ethereal Plane or a plane that doesn't border it, such as one of the Outer Planes.
Etherealness.entriesHigherLevel.0.entrie: You can target up to three willing creatures (including yourself) for each spell slot level above 7. The creatures must be within 10 feet of you when you cast the spell.
Etherealness.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Etherealness.name: Etherealness
Evard's Black Tentacles.entries.0: Squirming, ebony tentacles fill a 20-foot square on ground that you can see within range. For the duration, these tentacles turn the ground in that area into {@variantrule Difficult Terrain|XPHB}.
Evard's Black Tentacles.entries.1: Each creature in that area makes a Strength saving throw. On a failed save, it takes {@damage 3d6} Bludgeoning damage, and it has the {@condition Restrained|XPHB} condition until the spell ends. A creature also makes that save if it enters the area or ends it turn there. A creature makes that save only once per turn.
Evard's Black Tentacles.entries.2: A {@condition Restrained|XPHB} creature can take an action to make a Strength ({@skill Athletics|XPHB}) check against your spell save DC, ending the condition on itself on a success.
Evard's Black Tentacles.name: Evard's Black Tentacles
Expeditious Retreat.entries.0: You take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action, and until the spell ends, you can take that action again as a {@variantrule Bonus Action|XPHB}.
Expeditious Retreat.name: Expeditious Retreat
Extract Iron.entries.0: As part of casting this spell, you must expend three {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice} or the spell automatically fails. Roll the expended {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Dice} and extract a number of pounds of iron from your blood equal to the roll's total.
Extract Iron.entries.1: You fashion this iron into nonmagical metal objects. The combined weight of these objects must be equal to or less than the number rolled on the expended {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB}. The quality of these created objects is poor but functional.
Extract Iron.entriesHigherLevel.0.entrie: You can expend an additional {@variantrule Hit Point Dice|XPHB|Hit Point Die} and create additional iron for each spell slot level above 3.
Extract Iron.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
Extract Iron.name: Extract Iron
Eye Burn.entries.0: You sting the eyes of a creature attacking you. The target must make a Constitution saving throw (a creature with {@sense Blindsight|XPHB} or {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition succeeds automatically). On a failed save, the target has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} condition until the end of the turn. On a successful save, it must subtract {@dice 1d4} from the triggering attack roll. The target automatically succeeds on its saves against your subsequent casting of this spell for 24 hours.
Eye Burn.name: Eye Burn
Eyebite.entries.0: For the duration, your eyes become an inky void. One creature of your choice within 60 feet of you that you can see must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or be affected by one of the following effects of your choice for the duration.
Eyebite.entries.1: On each of your turns until the spell ends, you can take a {@action Magic|XPHB} action to target another creature but can't target a creature again if it has succeeded on a save against this casting of the spell.
Eyebite.entries.2.entries.0: The target has the {@condition Unconscious|XPHB} condition. It wakes up if it takes any damage or if another creature takes an action to shake it awake.
Eyebite.entries.2.name: Asleep
Eyebite.entries.3.entries.0: The target has the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition. On each of its turns, the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} target must take the {@action Dash|XPHB} action and move away from you by the safest and shortest route available. If the target moves to a space at least 60 feet away from you where it can't see you, this effect ends.
Eyebite.entries.3.name: Panicked
Eyebite.entries.4.entries.0: The target has the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.
Eyebite.entries.4.name: Sickened
Eyebite.name: Eyebite
Fabricate.entries.0: You convert raw materials into products of the same material. For example, you can fabricate a wooden bridge from a clump of trees, a rope from a patch of hemp, or clothes from flax or wool.
Fabricate.entries.1: Choose raw materials that you can see within range. You can fabricate a Large or smaller object (contained within a 10-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} or eight connected 5-foot Cubes) given a sufficient quantity of material. If you're working with metal, stone, or another mineral substance, however, the fabricated object can be no larger than Medium (contained within a 5-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube}). The quality of any fabricated objects is based on the quality of the raw materials.
Fabricate.entries.2: Creatures and magic items can't be created by this spell. You also can't use it to create items that require a high degree of skill—such as weapons and armor—unless you have proficiency with the type of {@item Artisan's Tools|XPHB} used to craft such objects.
Fabricate.name: Fabricate
Faerie Fire.entries.0: Objects in a 20-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} within range are outlined in blue, green, or violet light (your choice). Each creature in the {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} is also outlined if it fails a Dexterity saving throw. For the duration, objects and affected creatures shed {@variantrule Dim Light|XPHB} in a 10-foot radius and can't benefit from the {@condition Invisible|XPHB} condition.
Faerie Fire.entries.1: {@action Attack|XPHB} rolls against an affected creature or object have {@variantrule Advantage|XPHB} if the attacker can see it.
Faerie Fire.name: Faerie Fire
You gain 5 additional {@variantrule Temporary Hit Points|XPHB} for each spell slot level above 1.
False Life.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: Используя ячейку заклинания большего уровня
False Life.name: False Life
Far Step.entries.0: You teleport up to 60 feet to an unoccupied space you can see. On each of your turns before the spell ends, you can use a bonus action to teleport in this way again.
Far Step.name: Far Step
Fast Friends.entries.0: When you need to make sure something gets done, you can't rely on vague promises, sworn oaths, or binding contracts of employment. When you cast this spell, choose one humanoid within range that can see and hear you, and that can understand you. The creature must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or become {@condition charmed} by you for the duration. While the creature is {@condition charmed} in this way, it undertakes to perform any services or activities you ask of it in a friendly manner, to the best of its ability.
Fast Friends.entries.1: You can set the creature new tasks when a previous task is completed, or if you decide to end its current task. If the service or activity might cause harm to the creature, or if it conflicts with the creature's normal activities and desires, the creature can make another Wisdom saving throw to try to end the effect. This save is made with advantage if you or your companions are fighting the creature. If the activity would result in certain death for the creature, the spell ends.
Fast Friends.entries.2: When the spell ends, the creature knows it was {@condition charmed} by you.
Fast Friends.entriesHigherLevel.0.entrie: When you cast this spell using a spell slot of 4th level or higher, you can target one additional creature for each slot level above 3rd.
Fast Friends.entriesHigherLevel.0.name: На более высоких уровнях
Fast Friends.name: Fast Friends
Fear.entries.0: Each creature in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cone [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cone} must succeed on a Wisdom saving throw or drop whatever it is holding and have the {@condition Frightened|XPHB} condition for the duration.
Fear.entries.1: A {@condition Frightened|XPHB} creature takes the {@action Dash|XPHB} action and moves away from you by the safest route on each of its turns unless there is nowhere to move. If the creature ends its turn in a space where it doesn't have line of sight to you, the creature makes a Wisdom saving throw. On a successful save, the spell ends on that creature.
Fear.name: Страх
Feather Fall.components.m: a small feather or piece of down
Feather Fall.entries.0: Choose up to five falling creatures within range. A falling creature's rate of descent slows to 60 feet per round until the spell ends. If a creature lands before the spell ends, the creature takes no damage from the fall, and the spell ends for that creature.
Feather Fall.name: Feather Fall
Feign Death.entries.0: You touch a willing creature and put it into a cataleptic state that is indistinguishable from death.
Feign Death.entries.1: For the duration, the target appears dead to outward inspection and to spells used to determine the target's status. The target has the {@condition Blinded|XPHB} and {@condition Incapacitated|XPHB} conditions, and its {@variantrule Speed|XPHB} is 0.
Feign Death.entries.2: The target also has {@variantrule Resistance|XPHB} to all damage except Psychic damage, and it has {@variantrule Immunity|XPHB} to the {@condition Poisoned|XPHB} condition.
Feign Death.name: Feign Death
Field of Reaping.components.m: a dried corn husk or wheat hull
Field of Reaping.entries.0: You create a shadowy field that reaps escaping life force in a 30-foot {@variantrule Cube [Area of Effect]|XPHB|Cube} originating from a point within range. A creature {@variantrule Hostile [Attitude]|XPHB|Hostile} to you in the field when it appears, or when it enters the field for the first time on a turn or ends its turn there, is cursed until the end of its next turn.
